title: SAL-01 — Cash Sale
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-01 Cash Sale
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: Goods or services sold and payment received immediately in cash, bank, or wallet
Nepali meaning: सामान वा सेवा बेचेर तुरुन्तै नगद, बैंक वा वालेटबाट पैसा प्राप्त हुने
Romanized examples: aja 5000 ko bikri bhayo cash ma, cash ma becheko 2000
English examples: Sold goods for Rs.5000 cash today, Cash sale of Rs.2000
Required fields: amount, payment_mode
Optional fields: date, bill_number, item_list, customer_name_if_walkin_known
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Wallet [amount] / Cr Sales Revenue [amount]
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory by quantity sold at cost; increase COGS
Tax rule placeholder: Determine if VAT applies based on registration status and item category; confirm current VAT rate with IRD Nepal before including in bill
Clarification questions:
  - Payment mode cash, bank, or wallet ho?
  - Bill VAT sahit ho ki non-VAT ho?
Example user inputs: aja 5000 ko bikri bhayo cash ma, becheko maal 3000 ko esewa bata
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: cash_sale, debit: [Cash/Bank/Wallet], credit: [Sales Revenue], amount: 5000, inventory_effect: reduce, tax_effect: pending_confirmation}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Wallet [amount] / Cr Sales Revenue [amount]
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory by quantity sold at cost; increase COGS
tax_rule_placeholder: Determine if VAT applies based on registration status and item category; confirm current VAT rate with IRD Nepal before including in bill

title: SAL-02 — Credit Sale
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-02 Credit Sale
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: Goods or services sold to a customer without immediate payment, creating a receivable
Nepali meaning: ग्राहकलाई उधारोमा सामान वा सेवा दिइने, प्राप्य रकम सिर्जना हुने
Romanized examples: ram lai 5000 ko udharo diyo, customer lai credit ma maal diye
English examples: Sold goods on credit to Mr. Sharma for Rs.7000, Gave goods to Ram on credit worth Rs.5000
Required fields: amount, customer_name
Optional fields: date, due_date, bill_number, item_list
Journal entry rule: Dr Accounts Receivable (Customer) [amount] / Cr Sales Revenue [amount]
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory by quantity sold at cost; increase COGS
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm VAT applicability and rate before finalizing invoice value
Clarification questions:
  - Kun customer lai diyeko ho, naam ke ho?
  - Payment kahile aune expected cha?
Example user inputs: ram lai 5000 ko saman udharo diyo, Sharma lai 7000 ko credit ma becheko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: credit_sale, debit: [Accounts Receivable - Ram], credit: [Sales Revenue], amount: 5000, inventory_effect: reduce, tax_effect: pending_confirmation}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Accounts Receivable (Customer) [amount] / Cr Sales Revenue [amount]
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory by quantity sold at cost; increase COGS
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm VAT applicability and rate before finalizing invoice value

title: SAL-03 — VAT Sale
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-03 VAT Sale
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: A sale where a formal VAT invoice is issued and VAT is charged on top of or included in the price
Nepali meaning: भ्याट बिल जारी गरिने बिक्री, जसमा भ्याट थपिने वा समावेश हुने
Romanized examples: vat bill banau yo bikri ko, vat sahit bill katiyo 11300 ko
English examples: Please generate a VAT bill for this sale, Issued a VAT-inclusive invoice for Rs.11,300
Required fields: gross_or_net_amount, vat_registration_status
Optional fields: vat_rate, bill_number, customer_pan
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Receivable [gross amount] / Cr Sales Revenue [net amount] / Cr VAT Payable [VAT amount]
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory by quantity sold at cost
Tax rule placeholder: VAT rate and applicability must be confirmed from the current Finance Act and IRD Nepal notices; do not assume a fixed percentage
Clarification questions:
  - Yo business VAT registered ho?
  - Stated amount VAT inclusive ho ki exclusive ho?
Example user inputs: vat bill banau yo bikri ko, aja vat sahit bill katiyo 11300 ko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: vat_sale, debit: [Cash/Bank/Receivable], credit: [Sales Revenue, VAT Payable], amount: 11300, tax_effect: rate_pending_confirmation}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Receivable [gross amount] / Cr Sales Revenue [net amount] / Cr VAT Payable [VAT amount]
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory by quantity sold at cost
tax_rule_placeholder: VAT rate and applicability must be confirmed from the current Finance Act and IRD Nepal notices; do not assume a fixed percentage

title: SAL-04 — Non-VAT Sale
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-04 Non-VAT Sale
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: A sale recorded with only a PAN bill or plain receipt, no VAT charged
Nepali meaning: पान बिल वा सामान्य रसिद मात्र दिइने बिक्री, भ्याट नलागिने
Romanized examples: pan bill matra diyeko cha vat chaina, non vat bill ma becheko
English examples: Issued a plain PAN bill, no VAT applied, Non-VAT retail sale of Rs.2500
Required fields: amount
Optional fields: bill_number, customer_pan
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Receivable [amount] / Cr Sales Revenue [amount]
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory by quantity sold at cost
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm whether business is below VAT threshold or item is VAT-exempt per current IRD rules before treating as non-VAT
Clarification questions:
  - Business VAT registered ho ki chaina?
  - Yo item VAT-exempt category ma parcha ki pardaina?
Example user inputs: pan bill matra diyeko cha vat chaina, non vat ma becheko 2500
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: non_vat_sale, debit: [Cash/Bank/Receivable], credit: [Sales Revenue], amount: 2500, tax_effect: no_vat_applied}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Receivable [amount] / Cr Sales Revenue [amount]
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory by quantity sold at cost
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm whether business is below VAT threshold or item is VAT-exempt per current IRD rules before treating as non-VAT

title: SAL-05 — Mixed Sale
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-05 Mixed Sale
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: A single sale settled across multiple payment modes such as part cash, part card, part credit
Nepali meaning: एउटै बिक्री बिभिन्न भुक्तानी माध्यम (नगद, कार्ड, उधारो) मा विभाजित हुने
Romanized examples: half cash half credit ma becheko, cash bank esewa mix ma bikri bhayo
English examples: Sold goods for Rs.55,000, half cash half credit, Total sale split across cash, bank, and wallet
Required fields: total_amount, payment_split_by_mode
Optional fields: customer_name, bill_number
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash [portion] / Dr Bank [portion] / Dr Wallet [portion] / Dr Accounts Receivable [credit portion] / Cr Sales Revenue [total amount]
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory by total quantity sold at cost
Tax rule placeholder: Apply VAT treatment uniformly to total sale value once VAT status is confirmed
Clarification questions:
  - Exact split kati-kati ho cash, bank, wallet, credit ma?
Example user inputs: 55000 ko sale half cash half credit, aja bikri cash 8000 bank 5000 esewa 2000 credit 3500
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: mixed_payment_sale, debit: [Cash, Bank, Wallet, Accounts Receivable], credit: [Sales Revenue], amount: 18500, clarification_needed: true}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash [portion] / Dr Bank [portion] / Dr Wallet [portion] / Dr Accounts Receivable [credit portion] / Cr Sales Revenue [total amount]
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory by total quantity sold at cost
tax_rule_placeholder: Apply VAT treatment uniformly to total sale value once VAT status is confirmed

title: SAL-06 — Service Sale
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-06 Service Sale
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: Income earned from providing a service such as repair, salon, or transport, generally with no inventory effect except consumed parts
Nepali meaning: मर्मत, सैलून, ढुवानी जस्ता सेवा प्रदान गरेर आम्दानी हुने
Romanized examples: repair charge liye 1500, haircut ko paisa liye 500
English examples: Charged Rs.1500 for a repair service, Collected Rs.500 for a haircut
Required fields: amount, payment_mode
Optional fields: customer_name, parts_used_and_cost
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Wallet/Receivable [amount] / Cr Service Income [amount]
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory only for any spare parts or consumables physically used during the service
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm VAT applicability for services, which may differ from goods under current VAT Act provisions
Clarification questions:
  - Kunai part/consumable use bhayo ki matra labor charge ho?
Example user inputs: mobile screen change gareko charge 800, service charge liyeko 1500 repair ko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: service_sale, debit: [Cash/Bank], credit: [Service Income], amount: 1500, inventory_effect: reduce_if_parts_used}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Wallet/Receivable [amount] / Cr Service Income [amount]
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory only for any spare parts or consumables physically used during the service
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm VAT applicability for services, which may differ from goods under current VAT Act provisions

title: SAL-07 — Sales Return
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-07 Sales Return
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: A customer returns previously sold goods, reversing part or all of the original sale
Nepali meaning: ग्राहकले पहिले किनेको सामान फिर्ता गर्ने, बिक्री आंशिक वा पूर्ण रूपमा उल्ट्याइने
Romanized examples: saman firta aayo customer bata, customer le return gareko 1200 ko
English examples: Customer returned goods worth Rs.1200, Sales return of Rs.800 processed
Required fields: amount, customer_name_or_original_bill
Optional fields: reason_for_return, refund_or_credit_note
Journal entry rule: Dr Sales Revenue [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank/Accounts Receivable [amount]
Inventory rule: Increase Inventory by returned quantity at cost, unless goods are damaged/unsellable in which case route to inventory write-off
Tax rule placeholder: If original sale had VAT, the return should reduce VAT Payable proportionally per current VAT Act provisions on credit notes
Clarification questions:
  - Original sale cash ho ki credit ho?
  - Goods resellable condition ma cha ki damaged?
Example user inputs: saman firta aayo customer bata, customer le return gareko 1200 ko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: sales_return, debit: [Sales Revenue], credit: [Cash/Bank/Accounts Receivable], amount: 1200, inventory_effect: increase_if_resellable}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Sales Revenue [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank/Accounts Receivable [amount]
inventory_rule: Increase Inventory by returned quantity at cost, unless goods are damaged/unsellable in which case route to inventory write-off
tax_rule_placeholder: If original sale had VAT, the return should reduce VAT Payable proportionally per current VAT Act provisions on credit notes

title: SAL-08 — Sales Discount
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-08 Sales Discount
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: A reduction given to the customer off the original sale price at the point of sale
Nepali meaning: बिक्रीको समयमा ग्राहकलाई दिइने मूल्य छुट
Romanized examples: discount diyera beche, 20% discount ma becheko
English examples: Sold after giving a discount to customer, Applied 20% discount on summer collection
Required fields: original_price, discount_amount_or_percent
Optional fields: final_net_amount, payment_mode
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank [net amount] / Dr Discount Allowed [discount amount, if tracked separately] / Cr Sales Revenue [gross amount]
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory by quantity sold at cost, unaffected by discount
Tax rule placeholder: VAT, if applicable, should generally be calculated on the net (post-discount) price per standard practice; confirm with CA
Clarification questions:
  - Original price kati thiyo ra discount kati diyeko ho?
  - Final net amount kati bhayo?
Example user inputs: discount diyera beche, 6000 ko cash ma tara discount 200 diyeko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: cash_sale_with_discount, debit: [Cash], credit: [Sales Revenue], amount: 5800, notes: discount_200_applied}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank [net amount] / Dr Discount Allowed [discount amount, if tracked separately] / Cr Sales Revenue [gross amount]
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory by quantity sold at cost, unaffected by discount
tax_rule_placeholder: VAT, if applicable, should generally be calculated on the net (post-discount) price per standard practice; confirm with CA

title: SAL-09 — Free Sample
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-09 Free Sample
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: Goods given away free of charge for promotional purposes, no revenue recorded
Nepali meaning: प्रचारको लागि नि:शुल्क दिइने सामान, कुनै आम्दानी दर्ता नहुने
Romanized examples: customer lai free sample diyeko 2 piece, promotion ko lagi free ma diye
English examples: Gave 2 free sample units to a customer, Distributed free samples for promotion
Required fields: quantity, cost_value
Optional fields: campaign_or_reason
Journal entry rule: Dr Advertisement/Promotion Expense [cost value] / Cr Inventory [cost value]; no Sales Revenue recorded
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory by quantity given away at cost
Tax rule placeholder: Free samples may have specific VAT treatment (deemed supply) under VAT Act; confirm with CA before finalizing
Clarification questions:
  - Kati quantity ra cost value kati ho sample ko?
Example user inputs: customer lai free sample diyeko 2 piece, promotion ko lagi free ma diye 5 piece
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: free_sample, debit: [Advertisement Expense], credit: [Inventory], amount: cost_value, revenue_impact: none}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Advertisement/Promotion Expense [cost value] / Cr Inventory [cost value]; no Sales Revenue recorded
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory by quantity given away at cost
tax_rule_placeholder: Free samples may have specific VAT treatment (deemed supply) under VAT Act; confirm with CA before finalizing

title: SAL-10 — Damaged Goods Sale
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-10 Damaged Goods Sale
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: Sale of goods at a reduced price because they are damaged or defective
Nepali meaning: बिग्रेको वा खराब भएको सामान घटाइएको मूल्यमा बेचिने
Romanized examples: kharab maal sasto ma becheko, damaged item half price ma diye
English examples: Sold damaged stock at a reduced price, Sold defective items at half price
Required fields: reduced_sale_amount, original_cost_value
Optional fields: quantity, reason
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank [reduced amount] / Cr Sales Revenue [reduced amount]
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory by quantity sold at original cost, which may create a lower gross margin or loss on this line
Tax rule placeholder: VAT, if applicable, is charged on the actual reduced sale price, not the original price
Clarification questions:
  - Reduced price kati ho, ra original cost kati thiyo?
Example user inputs: kharab maal sasto ma becheko 500 ma, damaged item half price ma diye
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: damaged_goods_sale, debit: [Cash], credit: [Sales Revenue], amount: 500, inventory_effect: reduce_at_original_cost}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank [reduced amount] / Cr Sales Revenue [reduced amount]
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory by quantity sold at original cost, which may create a lower gross margin or loss on this line
tax_rule_placeholder: VAT, if applicable, is charged on the actual reduced sale price, not the original price

title: SAL-11 — Scrap Sale
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-11 Scrap Sale
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: Sale of waste material, damaged packaging, or unusable stock at scrap value
Nepali meaning: खराब पोका, कबाड वा प्रयोग नहुने सामान स्क्र्याप मूल्यमा बेचिने
Romanized examples: purano samaan becheko kabadi lai, scrap becheko 600
English examples: Sold scrap material for Rs.600, Sold old cartons to a scrap dealer
Required fields: amount
Optional fields: item_description
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank [amount] / Cr Scrap Sales [amount]
Inventory rule: Remove any residual book value of the scrapped item from Inventory if it was still carried at a value
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm whether scrap sales attract VAT under current provisions
Example user inputs: purano samaan becheko kabadi lai 600, scrap becheko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: scrap_sale, debit: [Cash], credit: [Scrap Sales], amount: 600}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank [amount] / Cr Scrap Sales [amount]
inventory_rule: Remove any residual book value of the scrapped item from Inventory if it was still carried at a value
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm whether scrap sales attract VAT under current provisions

title: SAL-12 — Advance from Customer
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-12 Advance from Customer
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: Money received from a customer before goods or services are delivered, an unearned revenue liability
Nepali meaning: सामान वा सेवा दिनु अगावै ग्राहकबाट प्राप्त हुने रकम, अर्जित नभएको आम्दानी दायित्व
Romanized examples: booking token liyeko 5000, advance liyeko customer bata order confirm garna
English examples: Received advance payment of Rs.3000 for future order, Took Rs.5000 booking token from customer
Required fields: amount, customer_name, payment_mode
Optional fields: expected_delivery_date, order_reference
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Wallet [amount] / Cr Advance Received (Customer) [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect until goods are actually delivered
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm whether VAT liability arises at the time of advance receipt or only at delivery, per current VAT Act treatment
Clarification questions:
  - Kun customer bata ho, ra kunbata (cash/bank/wallet) received bhayo?
Example user inputs: booking token liyeko 5000, advance liyeko customer bata 3000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: customer_advance, debit: [Cash/Bank/Wallet], credit: [Advance Received], amount: 5000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Wallet [amount] / Cr Advance Received (Customer) [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect until goods are actually delivered
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm whether VAT liability arises at the time of advance receipt or only at delivery, per current VAT Act treatment

title: SAL-13 — Customer Payment Received
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-13 Customer Payment Received
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: Full collection of a previously outstanding receivable from a customer
Nepali meaning: ग्राहकबाट पहिलेको बाँकी रकम पूरै असुल हुने
Romanized examples: customer ko baki tirya pura, grahak le pura tirya
English examples: Received full outstanding payment from customer, Customer paid off their entire due
Required fields: amount, customer_name, payment_mode
Optional fields: original_invoice_reference
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Wallet [amount] / Cr Accounts Receivable [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable — VAT was already recognized at the time of original sale
Clarification questions:
  - Kun customer ho, ra kunbata (cash/bank/wallet) tireko ho?
Example user inputs: customer ko baki tirya pura 5000, Ram le sabai baki tirya
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: customer_payment_collection, debit: [Cash/Bank], credit: [Accounts Receivable], amount: 5000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Wallet [amount] / Cr Accounts Receivable [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable — VAT was already recognized at the time of original sale

title: SAL-14 — Partial Payment Received
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-14 Partial Payment Received
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: Collection of only part of a customer's outstanding due, with a remaining balance still owed
Nepali meaning: ग्राहकको बाँकी रकम मध्ये आंशिक रकम मात्र असुल हुने, बाँकी अझै रहने
Romanized examples: customer le aadha paisa diyo, 2000 out of 5000 due tireko
English examples: Received partial payment of Rs.2000 against Rs.5000 due, Customer paid half of the outstanding amount
Required fields: amount_received, customer_name
Optional fields: total_due_amount, payment_mode
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Wallet [amount received] / Cr Accounts Receivable [amount received]; remaining balance stays on Accounts Receivable
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable — VAT was already recognized at the time of original sale
Clarification questions:
  - Kul rakam kati thiyo ra aaja kati tireko ho?
Example user inputs: customer le aadha paisa diyo, 2000 out of 5000 due tireko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: partial_payment, debit: [Cash], credit: [Accounts Receivable], amount: 2000, clarification_needed: true}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Wallet [amount received] / Cr Accounts Receivable [amount received]; remaining balance stays on Accounts Receivable
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable — VAT was already recognized at the time of original sale

title: SAL-15 — Bad Debt Write-off
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — SAL-15 Bad Debt Write-off
Category: SAL (Sales)
English meaning: Writing off a customer's receivable as permanently uncollectible
Nepali meaning: ग्राहकको बाँकी रकम असुल नहुने भनेर स्थायी रूपमा खारेज गर्ने
Romanized examples: customer ko baki asul hune chaina write off garne, udharo dubyo
English examples: Writing off customer's due as bad debt, Customer's credit balance is uncollectible
Required fields: amount, customer_name
Optional fields: reason, last_contact_date
Journal entry rule: Dr Bad Debt Expense [amount] / Cr Accounts Receivable [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm whether bad debt is deductible for income tax purposes under current Income Tax Act provisions
Clarification questions:
  - Yo confirm garna aghi, sabai collection effort try gareko cha? Owner le explicitly confirm garcha yo write off garne?
Example user inputs: customer ko baki asul hune chaina write off garne, udharo dubyo Ram ko 3000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: bad_debt_writeoff, debit: [Bad Debt Expense], credit: [Accounts Receivable], amount: 3000, requires_explicit_confirmation: true}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Bad Debt Expense [amount] / Cr Accounts Receivable [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm whether bad debt is deductible for income tax purposes under current Income Tax Act provisions

title: PUR-01 — Cash Purchase
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — PUR-01 Cash Purchase
Category: PUR (Purchase)
English meaning: Goods or materials bought and paid for immediately in cash, bank, or wallet
Nepali meaning: सामान वा कच्चा पदार्थ किनेर तुरुन्तै नगद, बैंक वा वालेटबाट भुक्तानी गरिने
Romanized examples: cash ma maal kineko 5000, bank bata kharid gareko
English examples: Purchased goods for Rs.5000 cash, Bought inventory paying by bank
Required fields: amount, payment_mode
Optional fields: supplier_name, bill_number, item_list
Journal entry rule: Dr Inventory [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
Inventory rule: Increase Inventory by quantity purchased at cost
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm if bill is a valid VAT invoice to determine if Input VAT can be recorded separately
Clarification questions:
  - Kun supplier bata kineko ho?
Example user inputs: cash ma maal kineko 5000, bank bata kharid gareko 8000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: cash_purchase, debit: [Inventory], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 5000, inventory_effect: increase}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Inventory [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
inventory_rule: Increase Inventory by quantity purchased at cost
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm if bill is a valid VAT invoice to determine if Input VAT can be recorded separately

title: PUR-02 — Credit Purchase
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — PUR-02 Credit Purchase
Category: PUR (Purchase)
English meaning: Goods bought from a supplier without immediate payment, creating a payable
Nepali meaning: सप्लायरबाट उधारोमा सामान किनिने, तिर्नुपर्ने दायित्व सिर्जना हुने
Romanized examples: supplier bata udharo ma kineko 25000, maal kharid gareko credit ma
English examples: Purchased inventory worth Rs.25000 on credit, Bought goods from supplier on credit terms
Required fields: amount, supplier_name
Optional fields: due_date, bill_number, item_list
Journal entry rule: Dr Inventory [amount] / Cr Accounts Payable (Supplier) [amount]
Inventory rule: Increase Inventory by quantity purchased at cost
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm if bill is a valid VAT invoice with supplier PAN before recording Input VAT
Clarification questions:
  - Kun supplier bata kineko ho, ra VAT bill cha ki chaina?
Example user inputs: supplier bata udharo ma kineko 25000, maal kharid gareko credit ma
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: credit_purchase, debit: [Inventory], credit: [Accounts Payable], amount: 25000, inventory_effect: increase}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Inventory [amount] / Cr Accounts Payable (Supplier) [amount]
inventory_rule: Increase Inventory by quantity purchased at cost
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm if bill is a valid VAT invoice with supplier PAN before recording Input VAT

title: PUR-03 — VAT Purchase
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — PUR-03 VAT Purchase
Category: PUR (Purchase)
English meaning: A purchase supported by a valid VAT invoice, allowing input VAT to be claimed
Nepali meaning: मान्य भ्याट बिल सहितको खरिद, इनपुट भ्याट दाबी गर्न मिल्ने
Romanized examples: vat bill sahit kineko, vat lageko bill ma kharid gareko
English examples: Purchased with a valid VAT invoice, Bought goods with VAT included on the bill
Required fields: gross_amount, supplier_pan_and_vat_number
Optional fields: vat_rate, bill_number
Journal entry rule: Dr Inventory [net amount] / Dr VAT Receivable (Input VAT) [VAT amount] / Cr Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable [gross amount]
Inventory rule: Increase Inventory by quantity purchased at net (pre-VAT) cost
Tax rule placeholder: VAT rate and eligibility for input credit must be confirmed against current VAT Act and IRD Nepal rules
Clarification questions:
  - Bill ma supplier ko VAT registration number cha ki chaina?
Example user inputs: vat bill sahit kineko 11300, vat lageko bill ma kharid gareko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: vat_purchase, debit: [Inventory, VAT Receivable], credit: [Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable], amount: 11300, tax_effect: rate_pending_confirmation}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Inventory [net amount] / Dr VAT Receivable (Input VAT) [VAT amount] / Cr Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable [gross amount]
inventory_rule: Increase Inventory by quantity purchased at net (pre-VAT) cost
tax_rule_placeholder: VAT rate and eligibility for input credit must be confirmed against current VAT Act and IRD Nepal rules

title: PUR-04 — Non-VAT Purchase
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — PUR-04 Non-VAT Purchase
Category: PUR (Purchase)
English meaning: A purchase with only a plain PAN bill, no VAT charged and no input VAT claimable
Nepali meaning: पान बिल मात्र भएको खरिद, भ्याट नलागिने र इनपुट भ्याट दाबी गर्न नमिल्ने
Romanized examples: pan bill matra cha vat xaina, non vat bill ma kineko
English examples: This bill has only PAN, no VAT, Non-VAT purchase of goods
Required fields: amount
Optional fields: supplier_name, bill_number
Journal entry rule: Dr Inventory [full amount] / Cr Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable [full amount]
Inventory rule: Increase Inventory by quantity purchased at full invoice cost
Tax rule placeholder: No Input VAT recorded since the bill is not a valid VAT invoice
Example user inputs: pan bill matra cha vat xaina, non vat bill ma kineko 6000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: non_vat_purchase, debit: [Inventory], credit: [Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable], amount: 6000, tax_effect: no_input_vat}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Inventory [full amount] / Cr Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable [full amount]
inventory_rule: Increase Inventory by quantity purchased at full invoice cost
tax_rule_placeholder: No Input VAT recorded since the bill is not a valid VAT invoice

title: PUR-05 — Import Purchase
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — PUR-05 Import Purchase
Category: PUR (Purchase)
English meaning: Goods or machinery purchased from a foreign supplier, potentially subject to customs duty and other import charges
Nepali meaning: विदेशी सप्लायरबाट किनिने सामान वा मेसिनरी, भन्सार महसुल तथा अन्य आयात शुल्क लाग्न सक्ने
Romanized examples: china bata machine import gareko customs sahit, india bata maal aayeko
English examples: Imported machinery from China with customs duty, Goods arrived from an Indian supplier
Required fields: amount, country_of_origin
Optional fields: customs_duty_amount, exchange_rate, import_license_reference
Journal entry rule: Dr Inventory/Fixed Asset [base cost + customs duty + import charges] / Cr Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable [total amount]
Inventory rule: Increase Inventory or Fixed Assets at landed cost including all import charges
Tax rule placeholder: Customs duty, VAT on import, and any excise must be confirmed from Customs Department and IRD Nepal current rates
Clarification questions:
  - Customs duty ra other import charges alag cha ki total ma include cha?
  - Exchange rate kati use gareko ho?
Example user inputs: china bata machine import gareko customs sahit 800000, india bata maal aayeko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: import_purchase, debit: [Inventory/Fixed Asset], credit: [Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable], amount: pending_landed_cost_confirmation}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Inventory/Fixed Asset [base cost + customs duty + import charges] / Cr Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable [total amount]
inventory_rule: Increase Inventory or Fixed Assets at landed cost including all import charges
tax_rule_placeholder: Customs duty, VAT on import, and any excise must be confirmed from Customs Department and IRD Nepal current rates

title: PUR-06 — Purchase Return
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — PUR-06 Purchase Return
Category: PUR (Purchase)
English meaning: Goods previously purchased are returned to the supplier, reducing the payable or receiving a refund
Nepali meaning: पहिले किनेको सामान सप्लायरलाई फिर्ता गरिने, तिर्नुपर्ने रकम घटाइने वा फिर्ता पैसा पाइने
Romanized examples: supplier lai maal firta gareko, kharab maal firta
English examples: Returned defective goods to supplier, Purchase return of Rs.1500 worth of goods
Required fields: amount, supplier_name
Optional fields: reason, original_bill_reference
Journal entry rule: Dr Accounts Payable [amount] / Cr Purchase Return [amount]; if already paid, Dr Cash/Bank instead
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory by returned quantity at cost
Tax rule placeholder: If original purchase had Input VAT claimed, the return should reduce that Input VAT claim per current rules
Clarification questions:
  - Yo purchase already paid vaisakeko thiyo ki still payable ma cha?
Example user inputs: supplier lai maal firta gareko 1500, kharab maal firta gareko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: purchase_return, debit: [Accounts Payable], credit: [Purchase Return], amount: 1500, inventory_effect: reduce}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Accounts Payable [amount] / Cr Purchase Return [amount]; if already paid, Dr Cash/Bank instead
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory by returned quantity at cost
tax_rule_placeholder: If original purchase had Input VAT claimed, the return should reduce that Input VAT claim per current rules

title: PUR-07 — Purchase Discount
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — PUR-07 Purchase Discount
Category: PUR (Purchase)
English meaning: A reduction in the purchase price given by the supplier, either at the time of purchase or as a later rebate
Nepali meaning: सप्लायरले खरिद मूल्यमा दिने छुट, खरिदको समयमा वा पछि रिबेटको रूपमा
Romanized examples: supplier le discount diyo 500, chhut paye khareed ma
English examples: Supplier gave a discount of Rs.500, Received a purchase discount
Required fields: original_amount, discount_amount
Optional fields: supplier_name
Journal entry rule: Dr Inventory/Accounts Payable [net amount after discount] / Cr Discount Received [discount amount, if tracked separately] / Cr Cash/Bank [net amount paid]
Inventory rule: Increase Inventory at net cost after discount
Tax rule placeholder: VAT, if applicable, is typically calculated on the discounted (net) purchase price
Clarification questions:
  - Original price kati thiyo ra discount kati paye?
Example user inputs: supplier le discount diyo 500, chhut paye khareed ma
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: purchase_discount, debit: [Inventory], credit: [Discount Received, Cash/Bank], amount: net_after_discount}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Inventory/Accounts Payable [net amount after discount] / Cr Discount Received [discount amount, if tracked separately] / Cr Cash/Bank [net amount paid]
inventory_rule: Increase Inventory at net cost after discount
tax_rule_placeholder: VAT, if applicable, is typically calculated on the discounted (net) purchase price

title: PUR-08 — Supplier Payment
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — PUR-08 Supplier Payment
Category: PUR (Purchase)
English meaning: Full payment made to a supplier to clear an outstanding accounts payable balance
Nepali meaning: सप्लायरको बाँकी दायित्व पूर्ण रूपमा चुक्ता गर्न गरिने भुक्तानी
Romanized examples: supplier lai paisa tireko pura, 15000 tirisakeko sahu lai
English examples: Paid supplier Rs.15000 through bank, Cleared supplier's full outstanding balance
Required fields: amount, supplier_name, payment_mode
Optional fields: bill_reference
Journal entry rule: Dr Accounts Payable [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable — Input VAT was already recognized at time of purchase; confirm any TDS withholding requirements for this payment type
Clarification questions:
  - Kun supplier lai ra kunbata (cash/bank) tireko ho?
Example user inputs: supplier lai paisa tireko pura 15000, sahu lai tirisakeko 15000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: supplier_payment, debit: [Accounts Payable], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 15000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Accounts Payable [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable — Input VAT was already recognized at time of purchase; confirm any TDS withholding requirements for this payment type

title: PUR-09 — Partial Supplier Payment
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — PUR-09 Partial Supplier Payment
Category: PUR (Purchase)
English meaning: Only part of a supplier's outstanding due is paid, with a balance still remaining owed
Nepali meaning: सप्लायरको बाँकी मध्ये आंशिक रकम मात्र तिरिने, बाँकी अझै रहने
Romanized examples: supplier lai aadha matra tireko, 12000 out of 20000 tireko
English examples: Paid Rs.12000 partial payment to supplier out of Rs.20000 due, Cleared half of supplier's outstanding balance
Required fields: amount_paid, supplier_name
Optional fields: total_due_amount, payment_mode
Journal entry rule: Dr Accounts Payable [amount paid] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount paid]; remaining balance stays on Accounts Payable
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable to the payment itself
Clarification questions:
  - Kul baki rakam kati thiyo ra aaja kati tireko ho?
Example user inputs: supplier lai aadha matra tireko, 12000 out of 20000 tireko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: partial_supplier_payment, debit: [Accounts Payable], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 12000, clarification_needed: true}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Accounts Payable [amount paid] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount paid]; remaining balance stays on Accounts Payable
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable to the payment itself

title: PUR-10 — Advance to Supplier
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — PUR-10 Advance to Supplier
Category: PUR (Purchase)
English meaning: Money paid to a supplier before the goods are actually received
Nepali meaning: सामान प्राप्त हुनु अगावै सप्लायरलाई तिरिने रकम
Romanized examples: supplier lai advance diye 50000, peshki tireko maal aauna agi
English examples: Paid Rs.50000 advance to supplier before goods arrive, Gave advance payment before receiving delivery
Required fields: amount, supplier_name, payment_mode
Optional fields: expected_delivery_date
Journal entry rule: Dr Advance to Supplier [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect until goods actually arrive, at which point Advance to Supplier is cleared against Inventory/Accounts Payable
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable until goods/bill are received
Clarification questions:
  - Kun supplier lai ra kunbata (cash/bank) diyeko ho?
Example user inputs: supplier lai advance diye 50000, peshki tireko maal aauna agi
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: advance_to_supplier, debit: [Advance to Supplier], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 50000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Advance to Supplier [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect until goods actually arrive, at which point Advance to Supplier is cleared against Inventory/Accounts Payable
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable until goods/bill are received

title: PUR-11 — Freight Inward
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — PUR-11 Freight Inward
Category: PUR (Purchase)
English meaning: Transport cost paid to bring purchased goods into the shop or warehouse, added to the cost of inventory
Nepali meaning: किनेको सामान पसल वा गोदाममा भित्र्याउन तिरिने ढुवानी खर्च, मौज्दातको लागतमा थपिने
Romanized examples: maal lyauda ko bhada tireko 3500, transport kharcha maal lyauna
English examples: Paid Rs.3500 transport cost to bring in goods, Freight expense for incoming stock
Required fields: amount
Optional fields: related_purchase_bill
Journal entry rule: Dr Inventory (added to cost) or Freight Inward [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
Inventory rule: Freight cost is capitalized into the cost of the goods received, increasing per-unit cost of Inventory
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm if transport service invoice carries VAT that can be claimed as input
Example user inputs: maal lyauda ko bhada tireko 3500, transport kharcha maal lyauna
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: freight_inward, debit: [Inventory/Freight Inward], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 3500}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Inventory (added to cost) or Freight Inward [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
inventory_rule: Freight cost is capitalized into the cost of the goods received, increasing per-unit cost of Inventory
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm if transport service invoice carries VAT that can be claimed as input

title: PUR-12 — Loading/Unloading Charge
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — PUR-12 Loading/Unloading Charge
Category: PUR (Purchase)
English meaning: Labor charges paid for loading or unloading purchased goods, typically added to inventory cost
Nepali meaning: किनेको सामान लोड/अनलोड गर्न तिरिने ज्याला, सामान्यतया मौज्दातको लागतमा थपिने
Romanized examples: loading unloading kharcha tireko 300, maal khasalna ra chadhauna kharcha
English examples: Paid Rs.300 for loading/unloading charges, Labor cost for unloading the delivery truck
Required fields: amount
Optional fields: related_purchase_bill
Journal entry rule: Dr Inventory (added to cost) [amount] / Cr Cash [amount]
Inventory rule: Loading/unloading cost is capitalized into the cost of the goods received
Tax rule placeholder: Generally a labor charge without VAT, but confirm if an invoice is issued by a formal labor contractor
Example user inputs: loading unloading kharcha tireko 300, maal khasalna ra chadhauna kharcha 300
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: loading_unloading_charge, debit: [Inventory], credit: [Cash], amount: 300}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Inventory (added to cost) [amount] / Cr Cash [amount]
inventory_rule: Loading/unloading cost is capitalized into the cost of the goods received
tax_rule_placeholder: Generally a labor charge without VAT, but confirm if an invoice is issued by a formal labor contractor

title: PUR-13 — Purchase Without Bill
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — PUR-13 Purchase Without Bill
Category: PUR (Purchase)
English meaning: Goods physically received into stock but no formal invoice or bill has been provided by the supplier yet
Nepali meaning: सामान भौतिक रूपमा प्राप्त भएको तर सप्लायरले अझै औपचारिक बिल नदिएको
Romanized examples: mal aayo tara bill chaina, maal aayo bill pachi aaune
English examples: Goods received but no bill yet, Received delivery without an invoice
Required fields: estimated_amount, supplier_name
Optional fields: expected_bill_date
Journal entry rule: Provisional: Dr Inventory [estimated amount] / Cr Accounts Payable [estimated amount]; update with actual figures once bill arrives
Inventory rule: Increase Inventory at estimated cost as a provisional entry, requiring later reconciliation against the actual bill
Tax rule placeholder: Input VAT cannot be claimed until a valid VAT invoice is received
Clarification questions:
  - Maal ko estimated value kati ho, ra bill kahile aaune expected cha?
Example user inputs: mal aayo tara bill chaina, maal aayo bill pachi aaune
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: purchase_without_bill, debit: [Inventory], credit: [Accounts Payable], amount: estimated_pending_confirmation, status: provisional}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Provisional: Dr Inventory [estimated amount] / Cr Accounts Payable [estimated amount]; update with actual figures once bill arrives
inventory_rule: Increase Inventory at estimated cost as a provisional entry, requiring later reconciliation against the actual bill
tax_rule_placeholder: Input VAT cannot be claimed until a valid VAT invoice is received

title: PUR-14 — Purchase with Handwritten Bill
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — PUR-14 Purchase with Handwritten Bill
Category: PUR (Purchase)
English meaning: A purchase supported only by an informal handwritten receipt rather than a printed or system-generated invoice
Nepali meaning: छापिएको वा प्रणालीबाट बनेको बिलको सट्टा हातैले लेखिएको अनौपचारिक रसिद मात्र भएको खरिद
Romanized examples: hate lekheko bill matra cha, handwritten receipt ma kineko
English examples: Purchase supported only by a handwritten receipt, Bought goods with just a handwritten slip as proof
Required fields: amount, supplier_name
Optional fields: item_description
Journal entry rule: Dr Inventory [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable [amount]; flag for lower documentation confidence
Inventory rule: Increase Inventory by quantity purchased at the amount stated on the handwritten bill
Tax rule placeholder: A handwritten bill generally cannot support an Input VAT claim; confirm with CA whether it meets minimum documentation standards for expense deductibility
Clarification questions:
  - Yo bill ma supplier ko naam, date, ra signature spashta cha?
Example user inputs: hate lekheko bill matra cha 4000 ko, handwritten receipt ma kineko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: purchase_handwritten_bill, debit: [Inventory], credit: [Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable], amount: 4000, documentation_confidence: low}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Inventory [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable [amount]; flag for lower documentation confidence
inventory_rule: Increase Inventory by quantity purchased at the amount stated on the handwritten bill
tax_rule_placeholder: A handwritten bill generally cannot support an Input VAT claim; confirm with CA whether it meets minimum documentation standards for expense deductibility

title: EXP-01 — Rent Paid
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — EXP-01 Rent Paid
Category: EXP (Expense)
English meaning: Payment made for shop, office, or godown rent
Nepali meaning: पसल, अफिस वा गोदामको भाडा बापत तिरिने भुक्तानी
Romanized examples: pasal ko bhada tireko 8000, ghar bhada tireko
English examples: Paid shop rent of Rs.8000, Paid monthly office rent
Required fields: amount, payment_mode
Optional fields: landlord_name, period_covered
Journal entry rule: Dr Rent Expense [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm applicable TDS withholding rate on rent payments per current Income Tax Act provisions
Clarification questions:
  - TDS katera tireko ho ki gross ma tireko?
Example user inputs: pasal ko bhada tireko 8000, ghar bhada tireko 8000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: rent_expense, debit: [Rent Expense], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 8000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Rent Expense [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm applicable TDS withholding rate on rent payments per current Income Tax Act provisions

title: EXP-02 — Salary Paid
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — EXP-02 Salary Paid
Category: EXP (Expense)
English meaning: Actual cash or bank payment of wages/salary to staff
Nepali meaning: कर्मचारीलाई तलब/ज्याला बापत नगद वा बैंकबाट गरिने भुक्तानी
Romanized examples: staff ko talab diye 20000, kamdar lai jyala tireko
English examples: Paid staff salary of Rs.20000 in cash, Paid worker wages for the week
Required fields: amount, payment_mode
Optional fields: staff_name, period_covered
Journal entry rule: Dr Salary/Wages Expense [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect unless wages relate directly to production, in which case Direct Labor applies instead
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm SSF (Social Security Fund) contribution and any applicable TDS on salary per current rules
Example user inputs: staff ko talab diye 20000, kamdar lai jyala tireko 2000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: salary_paid, debit: [Salary/Wages Expense], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 20000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Salary/Wages Expense [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect unless wages relate directly to production, in which case Direct Labor applies instead
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm SSF (Social Security Fund) contribution and any applicable TDS on salary per current rules

title: EXP-03 — Salary Payable
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — EXP-03 Salary Payable
Category: EXP (Expense)
English meaning: Salary that has been earned by staff but not yet actually paid, recorded as a liability
Nepali meaning: कर्मचारीले कमाइसकेको तर अझै भुक्तानी नभएको तलब, दायित्वको रूपमा दर्ता हुने
Romanized examples: staff ko talab baki cha 20000, salary due cha aile samma tireko chaina
English examples: Staff salary of Rs.20000 is due but unpaid, Salary payable is outstanding this month
Required fields: amount, period_covered
Optional fields: staff_name
Journal entry rule: Dr Salary/Wages Expense [amount] / Cr Salary Payable [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Accrual does not itself trigger TDS; TDS typically applies at time of actual payment per current rules
Example user inputs: staff ko talab baki cha 20000, salary due cha
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: salary_accrual, debit: [Salary/Wages Expense], credit: [Salary Payable], amount: 20000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Salary/Wages Expense [amount] / Cr Salary Payable [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Accrual does not itself trigger TDS; TDS typically applies at time of actual payment per current rules

title: EXP-04 — Electricity Expense
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — EXP-04 Electricity Expense
Category: EXP (Expense)
English meaning: Payment or accrual of the electricity bill for the shop or office
Nepali meaning: पसल वा अफिसको बिजुली बिल बापत तिरिने वा दायित्व सिर्जना हुने
Romanized examples: light bill tireko 1500, bijuli bill aayo 850 baki
English examples: Paid electricity bill of Rs.1500, Electricity bill of Rs.850 is due
Required fields: amount
Optional fields: payment_status, period_covered
Journal entry rule: Dr Electricity Expense [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank (if paid) or Accounts Payable/Utility Payable (if due) [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not typically subject to TDS for a small shop's own consumption bill
Clarification questions:
  - Bill tirisakeko ho ki tirna baki cha?
Example user inputs: light bill tireko 1500, bijuli bill aayo 850 baki
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: electricity_expense, debit: [Electricity Expense], credit: [Cash/Bank or Payable], amount: 1500}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Electricity Expense [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank (if paid) or Accounts Payable/Utility Payable (if due) [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not typically subject to TDS for a small shop's own consumption bill

title: EXP-05 — Water Expense
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — EXP-05 Water Expense
Category: EXP (Expense)
English meaning: Payment for the water utility bill of the shop or office
Nepali meaning: पसल वा अफिसको पानी महसुल बापत तिरिने भुक्तानी
Romanized examples: khanepani bill tireko 300, pani bill tirna baki
English examples: Paid water bill of Rs.300, Water bill is due this month
Required fields: amount
Optional fields: payment_status
Journal entry rule: Dr Water Expense [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank or Payable [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable
Example user inputs: khanepani bill tireko 300, pani bill tirna baki 300
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: water_expense, debit: [Water Expense], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 300}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Water Expense [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank or Payable [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable

title: EXP-06 — Internet Expense
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — EXP-06 Internet Expense
Category: EXP (Expense)
English meaning: Payment for internet/wifi service used by the business
Nepali meaning: व्यवसायले प्रयोग गर्ने इन्टरनेट/वाइफाई सेवा बापत तिरिने भुक्तानी
Romanized examples: wifi bill tireko 1200, internet bill payment gareko
English examples: Paid internet bill of Rs.1200, Paid for wifi subscription
Required fields: amount
Optional fields: provider_name
Journal entry rule: Dr Internet/Phone Expense [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable for standard consumption
Example user inputs: wifi bill tireko 1200, internet bill payment gareko 1200
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: internet_expense, debit: [Internet/Phone Expense], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 1200}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Internet/Phone Expense [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable for standard consumption

title: EXP-07 — Phone Expense
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — EXP-07 Phone Expense
