Nepali meaning: सप्लायर वा अन्य प्राप्तकर्तालाई चेक लेखेर दिइएको, तर अझै क्लियर नभएको हुन सक्ने
Romanized examples: cheque diye supplier lai 45000, cheque katera tireko
English examples: Issued a cheque of Rs.45000 to supplier, Gave a cheque payment
Required fields: amount, payee_name, cheque_number
Optional fields: expected_clearance_date
Journal entry rule: Dr Accounts Payable/Expense [amount] / Cr Bank Account [amount] (or Cr Cheques Payable if tracking uncleared cheques separately)
Inventory rule: No inventory effect directly, though if the cheque is for a purchase, Inventory increases per PUR-02
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable to the payment mechanism itself
Clarification questions:
  - Cheque number ke ho ra kaslai diyeko ho?
Example user inputs: cheque diye supplier lai 45000, cheque katera tireko 45000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: cheque_issued, debit: [Accounts Payable], credit: [Bank Account], amount: 45000, reference: cheque_number}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Accounts Payable/Expense [amount] / Cr Bank Account [amount] (or Cr Cheques Payable if tracking uncleared cheques separately)
inventory_rule: No inventory effect directly, though if the cheque is for a purchase, Inventory increases per PUR-02
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable to the payment mechanism itself

title: CB-05 — Cheque Received
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — CB-05 Cheque Received
Category: CB (Cash/Bank)
English meaning: A cheque has been received from a customer, which may still be pending bank clearance
Nepali meaning: ग्राहकबाट चेक प्राप्त भएको, जो अझै बैंक क्लियरेन्समा बाँकी हुन सक्ने
Romanized examples: customer le cheque diyo 8000, cheque paye ma
English examples: Received a cheque of Rs.8000 from customer, Customer gave a cheque payment
Required fields: amount, customer_name, cheque_number
Optional fields: expected_clearance_date
Journal entry rule: Dr Cheques in Hand (uncleared) [amount] / Cr Accounts Receivable [amount]; upon clearance, Dr Bank Account / Cr Cheques in Hand
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable to the payment mechanism itself
Clarification questions:
  - Cheque number ke ho ra kun customer bata ho?
Example user inputs: customer le cheque diyo 8000, cheque paye ma 8000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: cheque_received, debit: [Cheques in Hand], credit: [Accounts Receivable], amount: 8000, status: pending_clearance}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cheques in Hand (uncleared) [amount] / Cr Accounts Receivable [amount]; upon clearance, Dr Bank Account / Cr Cheques in Hand
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable to the payment mechanism itself

title: CB-06 — Cheque Bounced
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — CB-06 Cheque Bounced
Category: CB (Cash/Bank)
English meaning: A cheque that was previously recorded as cleared has failed to clear, requiring the original transaction to be reversed
Nepali meaning: पहिले क्लियर भएको मानिएको चेक क्लियर नभएको, मूल कारोबार उल्ट्याउनुपर्ने
Romanized examples: cheque bounce bhayo customer ko 8000, supplier lai diyeko cheque bounce vayo
English examples: Customer's cheque of Rs.8000 bounced, The cheque we issued to supplier bounced
Required fields: amount, party_name, cheque_number
Optional fields: bounce_charges
Journal entry rule: If customer cheque bounced: Dr Accounts Receivable [amount] / Cr Bank Account [amount]. If our cheque to supplier bounced: Dr Bank Account [amount] / Cr Accounts Payable [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable; any bank bounce penalty is a separate Bank Charges expense
Clarification questions:
  - Yo customer ko cheque ho ki hamile diyeko cheque ho?
  - Bounce charge lagyo ki lagena?
Example user inputs: cheque bounce bhayo customer ko 8000, supplier lai diyeko cheque bounce vayo
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: cheque_bounce, debit: [Accounts Receivable or Bank], credit: [Bank or Accounts Payable], amount: 8000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: If customer cheque bounced: Dr Accounts Receivable [amount] / Cr Bank Account [amount]. If our cheque to supplier bounced: Dr Bank Account [amount] / Cr Accounts Payable [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable; any bank bounce penalty is a separate Bank Charges expense

title: CB-07 — Wallet Receipt
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — CB-07 Wallet Receipt
Category: CB (Cash/Bank)
English meaning: Money received into a digital wallet such as eSewa, Khalti, Fonepay, or IME Pay
Nepali meaning: ईसेवा, खल्ती, फोनपे वा आइएमई पे जस्ता डिजिटल वालेटमा पैसा प्राप्त हुने
Romanized examples: esewa ma aayo paisa 3200, khalti bata payment aayo
English examples: Received Rs.3200 into eSewa, Payment received via Khalti
Required fields: amount, wallet_type
Optional fields: customer_name, purpose
Journal entry rule: Dr eSewa/Khalti/Fonepay/IME Pay Wallet [amount] / Cr Sales Revenue or Accounts Receivable [amount]
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory if this represents a new sale, per SAL-01 rules
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm VAT treatment if this is a sale, consistent with the applicable VAT rules for the underlying transaction
Clarification questions:
  - Yo naya sale ho ki purano due ko collection ho?
Example user inputs: esewa ma aayo paisa 3200, khalti bata payment aayo 800
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: digital_wallet_receipt, debit: [Wallet Account], credit: [Sales Revenue/Accounts Receivable], amount: 3200, clarification_needed: true}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr eSewa/Khalti/Fonepay/IME Pay Wallet [amount] / Cr Sales Revenue or Accounts Receivable [amount]
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory if this represents a new sale, per SAL-01 rules
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm VAT treatment if this is a sale, consistent with the applicable VAT rules for the underlying transaction

title: CB-08 — Wallet Payment
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — CB-08 Wallet Payment
Category: CB (Cash/Bank)
English meaning: Money paid out from a digital wallet to a supplier, expense, or other party
Nepali meaning: डिजिटल वालेटबाट सप्लायर, खर्च वा अन्य पक्षलाई पैसा तिरिने
Romanized examples: esewa bata supplier lai tireko, khalti bata payment gare
English examples: Paid supplier via eSewa, Made a payment through Khalti
Required fields: amount, wallet_type
Optional fields: payee_name, purpose
Journal entry rule: Dr Accounts Payable/Expense [amount] / Cr eSewa/Khalti/Fonepay/IME Pay Wallet [amount]
Inventory rule: Increase Inventory if this is a purchase payment, per PUR-01 rules
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable to the payment mechanism itself
Clarification questions:
  - Kaslai ra ke ko lagi tireko ho?
Example user inputs: esewa bata supplier lai tireko 5000, khalti bata payment gare 800
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: wallet_payment, debit: [Accounts Payable/Expense], credit: [Wallet Account], amount: 5000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Accounts Payable/Expense [amount] / Cr eSewa/Khalti/Fonepay/IME Pay Wallet [amount]
inventory_rule: Increase Inventory if this is a purchase payment, per PUR-01 rules
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable to the payment mechanism itself

title: CB-09 — Owner Cash Introduced
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — CB-09 Owner Cash Introduced
Category: CB (Cash/Bank)
English meaning: The owner puts personal money into the business as capital
Nepali meaning: मालिकले व्यक्तिगत पैसा व्यवसायमा पुँजीको रूपमा हाल्ने
Romanized examples: sahu le capital halyo 100000, malik le paisa halya
English examples: Owner invested Rs.100000 as capital, Owner put money into the business
Required fields: amount, payment_mode
Optional fields: date
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank [amount] / Cr Owner's Capital [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable — this is an equity injection, not income
Clarification questions:
  - Cash ma ho ki bank bata halya?
Example user inputs: sahu le capital halyo 100000, malik le paisa halya 100000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: owner_capital_injection, debit: [Cash/Bank], credit: [Owner's Capital], amount: 100000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank [amount] / Cr Owner's Capital [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable — this is an equity injection, not income

title: CB-10 — Owner Cash Withdrawn
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — CB-10 Owner Cash Withdrawn
Category: CB (Cash/Bank)
English meaning: The owner takes money out of the business for personal use, not a business expense
Nepali meaning: मालिकले व्यक्तिगत प्रयोगको लागि व्यवसायबाट पैसा झिकिने, यो व्यावसायिक खर्च होइन
Romanized examples: ghar kharcha ko lagi liyeko 5000, malik le paisa jhikeko
English examples: Owner withdrew Rs.5000 for personal use, Owner took money out of the shop
Required fields: amount, payment_mode
Optional fields: date
Journal entry rule: Dr Drawings [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable — this reduces owner's equity, not a business expense
Clarification questions:
  - Cash ma ho ki bank bata liyeko?
Example user inputs: ghar kharcha ko lagi liyeko 5000, malik le paisa jhikeko 5000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: owner_drawings, debit: [Drawings], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 5000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Drawings [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable — this reduces owner's equity, not a business expense

title: CB-11 — Cash Shortage
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — CB-11 Cash Shortage
Category: CB (Cash/Bank)
English meaning: An unexplained deficit found when the physical cash count is less than what the records show
Nepali meaning: भौतिक नगद गन्ती गर्दा रेकर्डमा भएको भन्दा कम भेटिने अस्पष्ट कमी
Romanized examples: cash counter tally garda 200 kam dekhiyo, till close garda 300 kam bhetiyo
English examples: Found Rs.200 short when tallying cash, Till was short by Rs.300 at closing
Required fields: amount
Optional fields: investigation_notes
Journal entry rule: After reasonable investigation, if unresolved: Dr Miscellaneous Expense (Cash Shortage) [amount] / Cr Cash in Hand [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable
Clarification questions:
  - Aajako sabai transaction pheri check gari shortage ko source pahichan garnu hola pahile posting garna
Example user inputs: cash counter tally garda 200 kam dekhiyo, till close garda 300 kam bhetiyo
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: cash_shortage, debit: [Miscellaneous Expense], credit: [Cash in Hand], amount: 200, requires_investigation_first: true}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: After reasonable investigation, if unresolved: Dr Miscellaneous Expense (Cash Shortage) [amount] / Cr Cash in Hand [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable

title: CB-12 — Cash Excess
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — CB-12 Cash Excess
Category: CB (Cash/Bank)
English meaning: An unexplained surplus found when the physical cash count is more than what the records show
Nepali meaning: भौतिक नगद गन्ती गर्दा रेकर्डमा भएको भन्दा बढी भेटिने अस्पष्ट अधिक्य
Romanized examples: cash counter tally garda 200 extra dekhiyo, till close garda 700 badhi bhetiyo
English examples: Found Rs.200 extra when tallying cash, Till had Rs.700 surplus at closing
Required fields: amount
Optional fields: investigation_notes
Journal entry rule: After reasonable investigation, if unresolved: Dr Cash in Hand [amount] / Cr Miscellaneous/Other Income [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable
Clarification questions:
  - Aajako sabai transaction pheri check gari surplus ko source pahichan garnu hola pahile posting garna
Example user inputs: cash counter tally garda 200 extra dekhiyo, till close garda 700 badhi bhetiyo
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: cash_excess, debit: [Cash in Hand], credit: [Miscellaneous/Other Income], amount: 200, requires_investigation_first: true}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: After reasonable investigation, if unresolved: Dr Cash in Hand [amount] / Cr Miscellaneous/Other Income [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable

title: INV-01 — Opening Stock
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — INV-01 Opening Stock
Category: INV (Inventory)
English meaning: The value of inventory on hand at the start of an accounting period
Nepali meaning: लेखा अवधि सुरु हुँदाको मौज्दातको मूल्य
Romanized examples: suru ko stock value 500000, opening stock set garne naya fiscal year ko lagi
English examples: Set opening stock value of Rs.500000, Recorded beginning inventory for new fiscal year
Required fields: amount, fiscal_period_start_date
Optional fields: item_wise_breakdown
Journal entry rule: Dr Opening Stock [amount] as part of COGS calculation; typically set once per fiscal year rather than a routine journal entry
Inventory rule: Establishes the starting inventory balance for the period
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable directly, though closing stock valuation method affects taxable profit
Clarification questions:
  - Fiscal year kun mitibata suru huncha (Shrawan 1 typically)?
Example user inputs: suru ko stock value 500000, opening stock set garne naya fiscal year ko lagi
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: opening_stock_setup, amount: 500000, period_start: fiscal_year_start}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Opening Stock [amount] as part of COGS calculation; typically set once per fiscal year rather than a routine journal entry
inventory_rule: Establishes the starting inventory balance for the period
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable directly, though closing stock valuation method affects taxable profit

title: INV-02 — Closing Stock
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — INV-02 Closing Stock
Category: INV (Inventory)
English meaning: The value of inventory remaining unsold at the end of an accounting period
Nepali meaning: लेखा अवधि सकिँदा नबिक्री रहेको मौज्दातको मूल्य
Romanized examples: antim stock count garda 300000 bhayo, year end ko closing stock value
English examples: Closing stock counted at Rs.300000, Year-end closing inventory value
Required fields: amount, fiscal_period_end_date
Optional fields: item_wise_breakdown
Journal entry rule: Dr Closing Stock (as Inventory asset) / Cr Closing Stock Adjustment (reducing COGS); typically a period-end calculated entry
Inventory rule: Establishes the ending inventory balance carried into the next period as opening stock
Tax rule placeholder: Inventory valuation method (FIFO, weighted average, etc.) affects taxable profit; confirm consistent method usage with CA
Clarification questions:
  - Physical stock count vaisakeko cha?
Example user inputs: antim stock count garda 300000 bhayo, year end ko closing stock value 300000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: closing_stock_setup, amount: 300000, period_end: fiscal_year_end}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Closing Stock (as Inventory asset) / Cr Closing Stock Adjustment (reducing COGS); typically a period-end calculated entry
inventory_rule: Establishes the ending inventory balance carried into the next period as opening stock
tax_rule_placeholder: Inventory valuation method (FIFO, weighted average, etc.) affects taxable profit; confirm consistent method usage with CA

title: INV-03 — Stock Adjustment Increase
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — INV-03 Stock Adjustment Increase
Category: INV (Inventory)
English meaning: A positive correction to inventory records, for example after finding more physical stock than the system shows
Nepali meaning: मौज्दात रेकर्डमा सकारात्मक सुधार, जस्तै प्रणालीमा भन्दा बढी भौतिक स्टक भेटिएको बेला
Romanized examples: stock count garda system bhanda 20 unit badi cha, extra stock bhetiyo
English examples: Physical count shows 20 more units than system, Found extra stock during counting
Required fields: quantity_or_value
Optional fields: reason, item_name
Journal entry rule: Dr Inventory [value] / Cr Miscellaneous/Other Income [value] (after investigation confirms genuine surplus)
Inventory rule: Increase Inventory quantity and value
Tax rule placeholder: Unexplained inventory gains may need review for correct income recognition
Clarification questions:
  - Extra stock ko source pata lagayeko cha ki chaina, confirm garna investigation garnu hola
Example user inputs: stock count garda system bhanda 20 unit badi cha, extra stock bhetiyo
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: stock_adjustment_increase, debit: [Inventory], credit: [Other Income], requires_investigation_first: true}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Inventory [value] / Cr Miscellaneous/Other Income [value] (after investigation confirms genuine surplus)
inventory_rule: Increase Inventory quantity and value
tax_rule_placeholder: Unexplained inventory gains may need review for correct income recognition

title: INV-04 — Stock Adjustment Decrease
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — INV-04 Stock Adjustment Decrease
Category: INV (Inventory)
English meaning: A negative correction to inventory records, for example after finding less physical stock than the system shows
Nepali meaning: मौज्दात रेकर्डमा नकारात्मक सुधार, जस्तै प्रणालीमा भन्दा कम भौतिक स्टक भेटिएको बेला
Romanized examples: stock count garda 5 piece missing dekhiyo, system bhanda kam stock cha
English examples: Stock take showed 5 pieces missing, Physical stock is less than system records
Required fields: quantity_or_value
Optional fields: reason, item_name
Journal entry rule: Dr Miscellaneous/Inventory Shrinkage Expense [value] / Cr Inventory [value] (after investigation)
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory quantity and value
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm deductibility of inventory shrinkage losses under current Income Tax Act provisions
Clarification questions:
  - Missing stock ko cost value kati ho, ra karan pata lagayeko cha ki chaina?
Example user inputs: stock count garda 5 piece missing dekhiyo, system bhanda kam stock cha
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: stock_adjustment_decrease, debit: [Inventory Shrinkage Expense], credit: [Inventory], requires_investigation_first: true}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Miscellaneous/Inventory Shrinkage Expense [value] / Cr Inventory [value] (after investigation)
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory quantity and value
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm deductibility of inventory shrinkage losses under current Income Tax Act provisions

title: INV-05 — Damaged Stock
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — INV-05 Damaged Stock
Category: INV (Inventory)
English meaning: Inventory that has become unsellable due to physical damage, requiring write-off
Nepali meaning: भौतिक रूपमा बिग्रिएर बिक्री गर्न नमिल्ने भएको मौज्दात, खारेज गर्नुपर्ने
Romanized examples: kharab bhayo maal 900 ko loss, fridge bigriyo maal chiso nabhayera bigriyo
English examples: Goods damaged, loss of Rs.900, Stock spoiled because the fridge broke
Required fields: value
Optional fields: reason, quantity
Journal entry rule: Dr Miscellaneous/Inventory Loss Expense [value] / Cr Inventory [value]
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory by the damaged quantity at cost
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm deductibility of damaged stock write-offs under current Income Tax Act provisions
Clarification questions:
  - Kati quantity ra value ko maal kharab bhayo?
Example user inputs: kharab bhayo maal 900 ko loss, fridge bigriyo maal chiso nabhayera bigriyo
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: damaged_stock_writeoff, debit: [Inventory Loss Expense], credit: [Inventory], amount: 900}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Miscellaneous/Inventory Loss Expense [value] / Cr Inventory [value]
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory by the damaged quantity at cost
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm deductibility of damaged stock write-offs under current Income Tax Act provisions

title: INV-06 — Expired Stock
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — INV-06 Expired Stock
Category: INV (Inventory)
English meaning: Inventory, typically perishable or medicine, that has passed its expiry date and must be written off
Nepali meaning: सामान्यतया चलनचल्तीका वा औषधि जस्ता मौज्दात जसको म्याद सकिएको हुन्छ र खारेज गर्नुपर्ने
Romanized examples: expiry medicine 5 box waste gare, dawai expiry vaisakyo firta
English examples: 5 boxes of expired medicine wasted, Medicine expired, needs write-off
Required fields: value
Optional fields: quantity, expiry_date, batch_number
Journal entry rule: Dr Miscellaneous/Inventory Loss Expense [value] / Cr Inventory [value]
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory by the expired quantity at cost
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm deductibility of expired stock write-offs, and whether any regulatory disposal requirements apply for pharmaceuticals
Clarification questions:
  - Kati quantity ra value ko expired medicine ho, batch number confirm garnu hola
Example user inputs: expiry medicine 5 box waste gare, dawai expiry vaisakyo firta
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: expired_stock_writeoff, debit: [Inventory Loss Expense], credit: [Inventory], amount: pending_value_confirmation}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Miscellaneous/Inventory Loss Expense [value] / Cr Inventory [value]
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory by the expired quantity at cost
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm deductibility of expired stock write-offs, and whether any regulatory disposal requirements apply for pharmaceuticals

title: INV-07 — Lost/Stolen Stock
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — INV-07 Lost/Stolen Stock
Category: INV (Inventory)
English meaning: Inventory that has disappeared due to theft or loss, requiring a write-off
Nepali meaning: चोरी वा हराएको कारणले हराएको मौज्दात, खारेज गर्नुपर्ने
Romanized examples: chor pasyo 20000 ko samaan lagyo, stock harayo pata lagena
English examples: Thief stole Rs.20000 worth of goods, Stock is missing, cause unknown
Required fields: value
Optional fields: police_report_reference, insurance_claim_status
Journal entry rule: Dr Miscellaneous/Theft Loss Expense [value] / Cr Inventory [value]
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory by the stolen/lost quantity at cost
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm deductibility of theft losses and interaction with any insurance claim recovery under current tax rules
Clarification questions:
  - Police report file bhayeko cha? Insurance claim garne planning cha?
Example user inputs: chor pasyo 20000 ko samaan lagyo, stock harayo pata lagena
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: theft_loss, debit: [Theft Loss Expense], credit: [Inventory], amount: 20000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Miscellaneous/Theft Loss Expense [value] / Cr Inventory [value]
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory by the stolen/lost quantity at cost
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm deductibility of theft losses and interaction with any insurance claim recovery under current tax rules

title: INV-08 — Stock Transfer
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — INV-08 Stock Transfer
Category: INV (Inventory)
English meaning: Inventory moved from one branch or location to another within the same business, not a sale
Nepali meaning: एउटै व्यवसाय भित्र एक शाखा वा स्थानबाट अर्को स्थानमा मौज्दात सारिने, बिक्री नभएको
Romanized examples: branch bata head office ma stock transfer gareko 50000, godown bata pasal ma maal sarya
English examples: Transferred Rs.50000 worth of stock from branch to head office, Moved goods from warehouse to shop
Required fields: value, from_location, to_location
Optional fields: quantity, item_list
Journal entry rule: Dr Inventory (Destination) [value] / Cr Inventory (Source) [value]; no revenue or expense impact
Inventory rule: No net change in total inventory, only relocation between tracked locations
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm if inter-branch transfers require any documentation under VAT Act if branches are separately registered
Clarification questions:
  - Branches eutai company ko ho ki separate legal entity ho?
Example user inputs: branch bata head office ma stock transfer gareko 50000, godown bata pasal ma maal sarya
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: inter_branch_stock_transfer, debit: [Inventory - Destination], credit: [Inventory - Source], amount: 50000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Inventory (Destination) [value] / Cr Inventory (Source) [value]; no revenue or expense impact
inventory_rule: No net change in total inventory, only relocation between tracked locations
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm if inter-branch transfers require any documentation under VAT Act if branches are separately registered

title: INV-09 — Unit Conversion
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — INV-09 Unit Conversion
Category: INV (Inventory)
English meaning: Converting inventory recorded in one unit of measure into another, such as bags into kilograms
Nepali meaning: मौज्दातलाई एक इकाईबाट अर्को इकाईमा रूपान्तरण गर्ने, जस्तै बोरालाई किलोग्राममा
Romanized examples: 50 kg chamal 1 bora ma convert gareko, bulk stock lai retail unit ma todeko
English examples: Converted 50 kg of rice into 1 bag unit, Broke down bulk stock into retail units
Required fields: source_unit_and_quantity, target_unit_and_quantity
Optional fields: item_name
Journal entry rule: No monetary journal entry typically required; this is a quantity/unit-of-measure adjustment within Inventory records
Inventory rule: Total inventory value remains the same; only the unit of measure and quantity representation changes
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable
Clarification questions:
  - Conversion ratio kati ho (jasto 1 bora = kati kg)?
Example user inputs: 50 kg chamal 1 bora ma convert gareko, bulk stock lai retail unit ma todeko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: unit_conversion, value_impact: none, quantity_recalculated: true}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: No monetary journal entry typically required; this is a quantity/unit-of-measure adjustment within Inventory records
inventory_rule: Total inventory value remains the same; only the unit of measure and quantity representation changes
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable

title: INV-10 — Free Quantity Received
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — INV-10 Free Quantity Received
Category: INV (Inventory)
English meaning: Extra stock given by a supplier at no additional cost, alongside a regular purchase
Nepali meaning: नियमित खरिदसँगै सप्लायरले कुनै थप शुल्क नलिई दिने अतिरिक्त मौज्दात
Romanized examples: supplier le bonus stock diyo free ma 10 piece extra, free sample supplier bata
English examples: Supplier gave 10 extra pieces free with the order, Received free promotional stock from supplier
Required fields: quantity
Optional fields: related_purchase_bill
Journal entry rule: No additional debit/credit entry for the free units themselves; effectively reduces the average unit cost of the total purchased quantity
Inventory rule: Increase Inventory quantity at zero incremental cost, reducing weighted average cost per unit
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm if free goods received have any specific VAT treatment as a deemed benefit
Clarification questions:
  - Yo kunai purchase order sanga bundled cha?
Example user inputs: supplier le bonus stock diyo free ma 10 piece extra, free sample supplier bata
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: free_stock_receipt, inventory_effect: increase_at_zero_incremental_cost}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: No additional debit/credit entry for the free units themselves; effectively reduces the average unit cost of the total purchased quantity
inventory_rule: Increase Inventory quantity at zero incremental cost, reducing weighted average cost per unit
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm if free goods received have any specific VAT treatment as a deemed benefit

title: INV-11 — Bonus Goods
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — INV-11 Bonus Goods
Category: INV (Inventory)
English meaning: Extra goods given to a customer as a promotional bonus alongside their purchase, such as buy-one-get-one offers
Nepali meaning: किनमेलसँगै ग्राहकलाई प्रचारात्मक बोनसको रूपमा दिइने अतिरिक्त सामान, जस्तै एक किन्दा एक फ्री अफर
Romanized examples: buy one get one free garya, bonus item diyeko customer lai
English examples: Ran a buy-one-get-one-free offer, Gave a bonus item to the customer
Required fields: paid_item_value, bonus_item_cost_value
Optional fields: campaign_name
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank [paid amount] / Cr Sales Revenue [paid amount]; reduce Inventory for both the paid and bonus items given
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory for the total quantity given away (paid item plus bonus item) at cost
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm VAT treatment for promotional bonus goods under current rules
Clarification questions:
  - Paid item ko price kati thiyo, ra bonus item ko cost value track garne ho?
Example user inputs: buy one get one free garya, bonus item diyeko customer lai
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: bonus_goods_promotion, debit: [Cash], credit: [Sales Revenue], inventory_effect: reduce_both_items}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank [paid amount] / Cr Sales Revenue [paid amount]; reduce Inventory for both the paid and bonus items given
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory for the total quantity given away (paid item plus bonus item) at cost
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm VAT treatment for promotional bonus goods under current rules

title: INV-12 — Manufacturing Consumption
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — INV-12 Manufacturing Consumption
Category: INV (Inventory)
English meaning: Raw material taken from inventory and used in the production process
Nepali meaning: उत्पादन प्रक्रियामा प्रयोग गरिने कच्चा पदार्थ मौज्दातबाट झिकिने
Romanized examples: raw material use gareko production ma 15000 ko, kaccha maal khard bhayo utpadan ma
English examples: Used Rs.15000 worth of raw material in production, Raw material consumed in manufacturing
Required fields: value
Optional fields: quantity, product_batch_reference
Journal entry rule: Dr Work-in-Progress or Cost of Goods Sold [value] / Cr Raw Material Inventory [value]
Inventory rule: Reduce Raw Material Inventory; increase Work-in-Progress or Finished Goods Inventory correspondingly
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable directly; affects cost basis used in COGS for tax reporting
Clarification questions:
  - Yo kun product batch ko lagi consumption ho?
Example user inputs: raw material use gareko production ma 15000 ko, kaccha maal khard bhayo utpadan ma
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: manufacturing_consumption, debit: [Work-in-Progress], credit: [Raw Material Inventory], amount: 15000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Work-in-Progress or Cost of Goods Sold [value] / Cr Raw Material Inventory [value]
inventory_rule: Reduce Raw Material Inventory; increase Work-in-Progress or Finished Goods Inventory correspondingly
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable directly; affects cost basis used in COGS for tax reporting

title: INV-13 — Finished Goods Production
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — INV-13 Finished Goods Production
Category: INV (Inventory)
English meaning: Completed products moved from work-in-progress into finished goods inventory ready for sale
Nepali meaning: उत्पादन प्रक्रिया पूरा भएर तयार सामान बिक्रीको लागि तयार मौज्दातमा सारिने
Romanized examples: 200 unit product banayeko cost track garne, finished product godown ma rakheko
English examples: Produced 200 units, need to record cost, Moved finished products into warehouse
Required fields: quantity, total_production_cost
Optional fields: batch_reference
Journal entry rule: Dr Finished Goods Inventory [total cost] / Cr Work-in-Progress [total cost]
Inventory rule: Increase Finished Goods Inventory by completed units at total production cost (raw material plus direct labor plus overhead)
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable directly; affects cost basis used in COGS for tax reporting
Clarification questions:
  - Raw material, direct labor, ra overhead ko total cost breakdown kati ho yo batch ko lagi?
Example user inputs: 200 unit product banayeko cost track garne, finished product godown ma rakheko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: finished_goods_production, debit: [Finished Goods Inventory], credit: [Work-in-Progress], amount: pending_cost_confirmation}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Finished Goods Inventory [total cost] / Cr Work-in-Progress [total cost]
inventory_rule: Increase Finished Goods Inventory by completed units at total production cost (raw material plus direct labor plus overhead)
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable directly; affects cost basis used in COGS for tax reporting

title: AST-01 — Fixed Asset Purchase
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — AST-01 Fixed Asset Purchase
Category: AST (Assets/Liabilities)
English meaning: Acquisition of a long-term item such as furniture, equipment, vehicle, or machinery for business use
Nepali meaning: फर्निचर, उपकरण, सवारी वा मेसिनरी जस्ता लामो अवधिसम्म प्रयोग हुने सामान व्यावसायिक प्रयोगको लागि खरिद गरिने
Romanized examples: furniture kineko 18000, computer kineko office ko lagi 45000
English examples: Purchased furniture worth Rs.18000, Bought a computer for the office
Required fields: amount, asset_type
Optional fields: payment_mode, useful_life_estimate
Journal entry rule: Dr [specific Fixed Asset account] [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable [amount]
Inventory rule: No effect on trade Inventory; this is a capital asset addition, not resale stock
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm applicable depreciation rate category under current Income Tax Act rules for tax purposes
Clarification questions:
  - Cash, bank, vaya credit ma kineko ho?
Example user inputs: furniture kineko 18000, computer kineko office ko lagi 45000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: fixed_asset_purchase, debit: [Furniture/Computer/Vehicle/Machinery], credit: [Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable], amount: 18000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr [specific Fixed Asset account] [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable [amount]
inventory_rule: No effect on trade Inventory; this is a capital asset addition, not resale stock
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm applicable depreciation rate category under current Income Tax Act rules for tax purposes

title: AST-02 — Fixed Asset Sale
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — AST-02 Fixed Asset Sale
Category: AST (Assets/Liabilities)
English meaning: Disposal of a fixed asset, potentially resulting in a gain or loss compared to its book value
Nepali meaning: स्थिर सम्पत्ति बिक्री गरिने, बुक भ्यालुको तुलनामा नाफा वा नोक्सान हुन सक्ने
Romanized examples: purano gadi becheko 200000 ma, old machine sale gareko
English examples: Sold old vehicle for Rs.200000, Sold an old machine
Required fields: sale_amount, asset_original_cost, accumulated_depreciation
Optional fields: buyer_name
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank [sale amount] / Dr Accumulated Depreciation [amount] / Cr Fixed Asset [original cost] / Cr or Dr Gain/Loss on Disposal [difference]
Inventory rule: No effect on trade Inventory
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm tax treatment of gain/loss on asset disposal under current Income Tax Act provisions
Clarification questions:
  - Asset ko original cost ra accumulated depreciation kati ho?
Example user inputs: purano gadi becheko 200000 ma, old machine sale gareko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: fixed_asset_disposal, debit: [Cash/Bank, Accumulated Depreciation], credit: [Fixed Asset, Gain on Disposal], amount: 200000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank [sale amount] / Dr Accumulated Depreciation [amount] / Cr Fixed Asset [original cost] / Cr or Dr Gain/Loss on Disposal [difference]
inventory_rule: No effect on trade Inventory
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm tax treatment of gain/loss on asset disposal under current Income Tax Act provisions

title: AST-03 — Depreciation
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — AST-03 Depreciation
Category: AST (Assets/Liabilities)
English meaning: The periodic charge representing the wear and use of a fixed asset over time
Nepali meaning: समयसँगै स्थिर सम्पत्तिको उपयोग र क्षयलाई प्रतिनिधित्व गर्ने आवधिक शुल्क
Romanized examples: depreciation calculate garna paryo year end aayo, machine ko hraskatti hisab garne
English examples: Need to calculate depreciation, year end has come, Calculate depreciation for the machine
Required fields: asset_name, depreciation_method_and_rate
Optional fields: useful_life_remaining
Journal entry rule: Dr Depreciation Expense [amount] / Cr Accumulated Depreciation [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm applicable depreciation rate/method under current Income Tax Act rules, which may differ from accounting depreciation for tax purposes
Clarification questions:
  - Kun fixed assets ko depreciation calculate garne ho, ra method/rate ke prayog garne ho?
Example user inputs: depreciation calculate garna paryo year end aayo, machine ko hraskatti hisab garne
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: depreciation_charge, debit: [Depreciation Expense], credit: [Accumulated Depreciation], amount: pending_calculation}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Depreciation Expense [amount] / Cr Accumulated Depreciation [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm applicable depreciation rate/method under current Income Tax Act rules, which may differ from accounting depreciation for tax purposes

title: AST-04 — Loan Received
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — AST-04 Loan Received
Category: AST (Assets/Liabilities)
English meaning: Funds borrowed from a bank, cooperative, or individual, creating a liability to repay
Nepali meaning: बैंक, सहकारी वा व्यक्तिबाट लिइने ऋण, फिर्ता तिर्नुपर्ने दायित्व सिर्जना हुने
Romanized examples: loan liyeko bank bata 50000, karja liyeko sahu bata
English examples: Took a loan of Rs.50000 from bank, Borrowed money from a moneylender
Required fields: amount, lender_name
Optional fields: interest_rate, repayment_terms
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank [amount] / Cr Loan Payable [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm any withholding requirements on interest to be paid on this loan later
Clarification questions:
  - Kun bank/sahu bata loan liyeko ho ra cash vaya bank ma aayo?
Example user inputs: loan liyeko bank bata 50000, karja liyeko sahu bata 50000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: loan_receipt, debit: [Cash/Bank], credit: [Loan Payable], amount: 50000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank [amount] / Cr Loan Payable [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm any withholding requirements on interest to be paid on this loan later

title: AST-05 — Loan Repayment
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — AST-05 Loan Repayment
Category: AST (Assets/Liabilities)
English meaning: Payment made to reduce the outstanding principal balance of a loan, which may also include an interest component
Nepali meaning: ऋणको बाँकी मूलधन घटाउन गरिने भुक्तानी, जसमा ब्याज अंश पनि समावेश हुन सक्ने
Romanized examples: loan installment tireko 5000 including interest, EMI tireko
English examples: Paid loan installment of Rs.5000 including interest, Paid EMI for the loan
Required fields: total_installment_amount
Optional fields: principal_portion, interest_portion
Journal entry rule: Dr Loan Payable [principal portion] / Dr Interest Expense [interest portion] / Cr Cash/Bank [total amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm interest deductibility limits under current Income Tax Act provisions
Clarification questions:
  - Kati principal ra kati interest cha yo installment ma?
Example user inputs: loan installment tireko 5000 including interest, EMI tireko 5000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: loan_repayment, debit: [Loan Payable, Interest Expense], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 5000, clarification_needed: true}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Loan Payable [principal portion] / Dr Interest Expense [interest portion] / Cr Cash/Bank [total amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm interest deductibility limits under current Income Tax Act provisions

title: AST-06 — Interest Accrued
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — AST-06 Interest Accrued
Category: AST (Assets/Liabilities)
English meaning: Interest that has built up on a loan but has not yet been actually paid, recorded as a liability
Nepali meaning: ऋणमा जम्मा भएको तर अझै तिरिनसकेको ब्याज, दायित्वको रूपमा दर्ता हुने
Romanized examples: byaj baki cha aile samma tireko chaina, interest accrue vayeko loan ma
English examples: Interest is due but not yet paid, Interest has accrued on the loan
Required fields: amount
Optional fields: loan_reference
Journal entry rule: Dr Interest Expense [amount] / Cr Interest Payable [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm timing of interest deductibility (accrual vs cash basis) under current Income Tax Act provisions
Example user inputs: byaj baki cha aile samma tireko chaina, interest accrue vayeko loan ma 500
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: interest_accrual, debit: [Interest Expense], credit: [Interest Payable], amount: 500}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Interest Expense [amount] / Cr Interest Payable [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm timing of interest deductibility (accrual vs cash basis) under current Income Tax Act provisions

title: AST-07 — Security Deposit Paid
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — AST-07 Security Deposit Paid
Category: AST (Assets/Liabilities)
English meaning: A refundable deposit paid by the business to a landlord or service provider
Nepali meaning: व्यवसायले घरबेटी वा सेवा प्रदायकलाई तिरेको फिर्ता हुने धरौटी
Romanized examples: pasal bhada ko deposit tireko 10000, dharauti diyeko landlord lai
English examples: Paid Rs.10000 rental security deposit, Gave a deposit to the landlord
Required fields: amount, recipient_name
Optional fields: purpose
Journal entry rule: Dr Security Deposit (Given) [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable — this is a refundable asset, not an expense
Example user inputs: pasal bhada ko deposit tireko 10000, dharauti diyeko landlord lai 10000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: security_deposit_given, debit: [Security Deposit], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 10000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Security Deposit (Given) [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable — this is a refundable asset, not an expense

title: AST-08 — Security Deposit Received
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — AST-08 Security Deposit Received
Category: AST (Assets/Liabilities)
English meaning: A refundable deposit received by the business from a customer or tenant
Nepali meaning: व्यवसायले ग्राहक वा भाडावालाबाट प्राप्त गरेको फिर्ता हुने धरौटी
Romanized examples: customer bata deposit liyeko rental ko lagi, dharauti paye customer bata
English examples: Received a rental deposit from customer, Took a deposit from tenant
Required fields: amount, payer_name
Optional fields: purpose
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank [amount] / Cr Security Deposit (Received) [liability account] [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable — this is a refundable liability, not income
Example user inputs: customer bata deposit liyeko rental ko lagi 5000, dharauti paye customer bata 5000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: security_deposit_received, debit: [Cash/Bank], credit: [Security Deposit Liability], amount: 5000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank [amount] / Cr Security Deposit (Received) [liability account] [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable — this is a refundable liability, not income

title: AST-09 — Advance Rent
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — AST-09 Advance Rent
Category: AST (Assets/Liabilities)
English meaning: Rent paid in advance covering a future period, treated as a prepaid asset until consumed
Nepali meaning: भविष्यको अवधि समेट्ने अग्रिम रूपमा तिरिने भाडा, उपयोग नभएसम्म पेश्की सम्पत्तिको रूपमा हुने
Romanized examples: ek saal ko bhada agadi tireko, 2 mahina ko rent advance tireko
English examples: Paid one full year's rent in advance, Paid 2 months' rent advance
Required fields: amount, period_covered
Optional fields: landlord_name
Journal entry rule: Dr Prepaid Expenses [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]; amortize to Rent Expense monthly over the covered period
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm timing of expense deductibility (matching period vs cash paid) under current Income Tax Act provisions
Clarification questions:
  - Kati mahina ko rent cover garcha yo advance le?
Example user inputs: ek saal ko bhada agadi tireko 96000, 2 mahina ko rent advance tireko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: advance_rent, debit: [Prepaid Expenses], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 96000, amortization: monthly_over_covered_period}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Prepaid Expenses [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]; amortize to Rent Expense monthly over the covered period
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm timing of expense deductibility (matching period vs cash paid) under current Income Tax Act provisions

title: AST-10 — Prepaid Expense
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — AST-10 Prepaid Expense
Category: AST (Assets/Liabilities)
English meaning: Any expense paid in advance for a future period, other than rent, such as insurance or annual subscriptions
Nepali meaning: भाडा बाहेक भविष्यको अवधिको लागि अग्रिम तिरिने कुनै खर्च, जस्तै बिमा वा वार्षिक सदस्यता
Romanized examples: insurance premium agadi tireko yearly, software subscription yearly tireko
English examples: Paid annual insurance premium in advance, Paid yearly software subscription upfront
Required fields: amount, period_covered
Optional fields: service_type
Journal entry rule: Dr Prepaid Expenses [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]; amortize to relevant Expense account monthly over the covered period
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm timing of expense deductibility under current Income Tax Act provisions
Clarification questions:
  - Kati mahina/saal ko period cover garcha yo payment le?
Example user inputs: insurance premium agadi tireko yearly 12000, software subscription yearly tireko 8000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: prepaid_expense, debit: [Prepaid Expenses], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 12000, amortization: monthly_over_covered_period}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Prepaid Expenses [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]; amortize to relevant Expense account monthly over the covered period
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm timing of expense deductibility under current Income Tax Act provisions

title: TAX-01 — VAT Output
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — TAX-01 VAT Output
Category: TAX (Tax/Compliance)
English meaning: VAT charged to a customer on a sale, recorded as a liability owed to the tax authority
Nepali meaning: ग्राहकबाट बिक्रीमा असुल गरिने भ्याट, कर प्राधिकरणलाई तिर्नुपर्ने दायित्वको रूपमा दर्ता हुने
Romanized examples: vat bill diyeko customer lai, output vat calculate garne bikri ko
English examples: Issued a VAT bill to customer, Calculating output VAT on this sale
Required fields: sale_amount, vat_rate
Optional fields: bill_number
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Receivable [gross amount] / Cr Sales Revenue [net amount] / Cr VAT Payable [VAT amount]
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory per the underlying sale, per SAL-01/SAL-02 rules
Tax rule placeholder: Current VAT rate must be confirmed from the latest Finance Act and IRD Nepal before calculation
Clarification questions:
  - Yo business VAT registered ho? Applicable VAT rate confirm garnu hola
Example user inputs: vat bill diyeko customer lai, output vat calculate garne bikri ko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: vat_output, credit: [VAT Payable], amount: rate_pending_confirmation}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank/Receivable [gross amount] / Cr Sales Revenue [net amount] / Cr VAT Payable [VAT amount]
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory per the underlying sale, per SAL-01/SAL-02 rules
tax_rule_placeholder: Current VAT rate must be confirmed from the latest Finance Act and IRD Nepal before calculation

title: TAX-02 — VAT Input
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — TAX-02 VAT Input
Category: TAX (Tax/Compliance)
English meaning: VAT paid on a purchase supported by a valid VAT invoice, claimable as a credit against VAT owed
Nepali meaning: मान्य भ्याट बिल सहित खरिदमा तिरिएको भ्याट, तिर्नुपर्ने भ्याटको विरुद्ध क्रेडिटको रूपमा दाबी गर्न मिल्ने
Romanized examples: input vat claim garna milcha ki mildaina yo bill le, vat lageko purchase bill
English examples: Can input VAT be claimed on this bill, VAT-included purchase invoice
Required fields: purchase_amount, supplier_vat_registration
Optional fields: bill_number
Journal entry rule: Dr Inventory/Expense [net amount] / Dr VAT Receivable (Input VAT) [VAT amount] / Cr Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable [gross amount]
Inventory rule: Increase Inventory at net (pre-VAT) cost, per PUR-01/PUR-02 rules
Tax rule placeholder: Bill must be a valid VAT invoice from a VAT-registered supplier; confirm eligibility per current IRD rules before claiming credit
Clarification questions:
  - Bill VAT-registered supplier ko proper VAT invoice format ma cha?
Example user inputs: input vat claim garna milcha ki mildaina yo bill le, vat lageko purchase bill
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: vat_input, debit: [VAT Receivable], amount: pending_bill_validity_confirmation}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Inventory/Expense [net amount] / Dr VAT Receivable (Input VAT) [VAT amount] / Cr Cash/Bank/Accounts Payable [gross amount]
inventory_rule: Increase Inventory at net (pre-VAT) cost, per PUR-01/PUR-02 rules
tax_rule_placeholder: Bill must be a valid VAT invoice from a VAT-registered supplier; confirm eligibility per current IRD rules before claiming credit

title: TAX-03 — VAT Adjustment
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — TAX-03 VAT Adjustment
Category: TAX (Tax/Compliance)
English meaning: Correction to previously recorded VAT figures, such as offsetting input VAT against output VAT during filing
Nepali meaning: पहिले दर्ता भएको भ्याट अंकमा सुधार, जस्तै फाइलिंगको समयमा इनपुट भ्याटलाई आउटपुट भ्याटसँग समायोजन गर्ने
Romanized examples: monthly VAT filing garna paryo input output milaune, VAT adjust garne mahina antya ma
English examples: Need to file monthly VAT, offsetting input and output, Adjusting VAT at month end
Required fields: input_vat_amount, output_vat_amount
Optional fields: filing_period
Journal entry rule: Dr VAT Payable [input VAT amount] / Cr VAT Receivable [input VAT amount], netting to determine amount due or refundable
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: VAT filing process and net payable/refundable calculation must follow current IRD Nepal procedures; confirm with CA
Clarification questions:
  - Yo mahina ko total input VAT ra output VAT kati ho?
Example user inputs: monthly VAT filing garna paryo input output milaune, VAT adjust garne mahina antya ma
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: vat_adjustment, debit: [VAT Payable], credit: [VAT Receivable], amount: net_pending_calculation}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr VAT Payable [input VAT amount] / Cr VAT Receivable [input VAT amount], netting to determine amount due or refundable
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: VAT filing process and net payable/refundable calculation must follow current IRD Nepal procedures; confirm with CA

title: TAX-04 — TDS Deducted by Customer
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — TAX-04 TDS Deducted by Customer
Category: TAX (Tax/Compliance)
English meaning: A customer has withheld tax at source from a payment owed to the business, reducing the cash actually received
Nepali meaning: ग्राहकले व्यवसायलाई तिर्नुपर्ने भुक्तानीबाट स्रोतमा कर कट्टा गरेको, वास्तवमा प्राप्त हुने नगद घट्ने
Romanized examples: customer le TDS katera tireko service charge ma, service payment ma TDS deduct bhayo
English examples: Customer deducted TDS from our service payment, TDS was deducted on the payment received
Required fields: gross_amount, tds_amount
Optional fields: tds_certificate_reference
Journal entry rule: Dr Cash/Bank [net amount] / Dr TDS Receivable [TDS amount] / Cr Service Income/Sales Revenue [gross amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect unless underlying transaction is a goods sale
Tax rule placeholder: TDS rate withheld should be confirmed against current Income Tax Act provisions applicable to the service/payment type
Clarification questions:
  - Gross amount kati thiyo ra TDS certificate paunu bhayo?
Example user inputs: customer le TDS katera tireko service charge ma, service payment ma TDS deduct bhayo
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: tds_deducted_by_customer, debit: [Cash/Bank, TDS Receivable], credit: [Service Income], amount: gross_pending_split}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Cash/Bank [net amount] / Dr TDS Receivable [TDS amount] / Cr Service Income/Sales Revenue [gross amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect unless underlying transaction is a goods sale
tax_rule_placeholder: TDS rate withheld should be confirmed against current Income Tax Act provisions applicable to the service/payment type

title: TAX-05 — TDS Deducted from Supplier
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — TAX-05 TDS Deducted from Supplier
Category: TAX (Tax/Compliance)
English meaning: The business withholds tax at source from a payment it makes to a supplier or service provider
Nepali meaning: व्यवसायले सप्लायर वा सेवा प्रदायकलाई गर्ने भुक्तानीबाट स्रोतमा कर कट्टा गर्ने
Romanized examples: rent tireko landlord lai TDS katera 2000 katya, professional fee ma TDS katya
English examples: Paid rent to landlord after deducting TDS of Rs.2000, Deducted TDS on professional fee payment
Required fields: gross_amount, tds_amount
Optional fields: payee_name
Journal entry rule: Dr Rent/Professional Fee Expense [gross amount] / Cr TDS Payable [TDS amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [net amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Applicable TDS rate must be confirmed against current Income Tax Act provisions for the specific payment category
Clarification questions:
  - Total gross rakam kati thiyo ra TDS rate kati lagayeko ho?
Example user inputs: rent tireko landlord lai TDS katera 2000 katya, professional fee ma TDS katya
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: tds_deducted_from_supplier, debit: [Expense], credit: [TDS Payable, Cash/Bank], amount: rate_pending_confirmation}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Rent/Professional Fee Expense [gross amount] / Cr TDS Payable [TDS amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [net amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Applicable TDS rate must be confirmed against current Income Tax Act provisions for the specific payment category

title: TAX-06 — TDS Payment
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — TAX-06 TDS Payment
Category: TAX (Tax/Compliance)
English meaning: The business deposits previously withheld TDS amounts with the tax authority
Nepali meaning: व्यवसायले पहिले कट्टा गरेको TDS रकम कर प्राधिकरणमा जम्मा गर्ने
Romanized examples: TDS jama gareko IRD ma, tax office ma TDS deposit gareko
English examples: Deposited TDS with tax office, Paid the withheld TDS to IRD

Optional fields: filing_period, receipt_reference

Tax rule placeholder: Confirm TDS deposit deadlines and process per current IRD Nepal procedures

Example ERP output: {transaction_type: tds_payment, debit: [TDS Payable], credit: [Bank Account], amount: 2000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr TDS Payable [amount] / Cr Bank Account [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect

title: TAX-07 — Income Tax Installment
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — TAX-07 Income Tax Installment
Category: TAX (Tax/Compliance)
English meaning: Advance payment of estimated annual income tax, typically paid in installments during the fiscal year
Nepali meaning: वार्षिक आयकरको अनुमानित रकम अग्रिम रूपमा तिरिने, सामान्यतया वित्तीय वर्षभरि किस्तामा तिरिने
Romanized examples: income tax installment tireko yo tirmaash ko, advance tax jama gareko
English examples: Paid this quarter's income tax installment, Deposited advance income tax
Required fields: amount, installment_period
Optional fields: receipt_reference
Journal entry rule: Dr Income Tax Advance (Asset) [amount] / Cr Bank Account [amount]; adjusted against final tax liability at year end
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Confirm installment schedule, due dates, and calculation basis per current Income Tax Act provisions with CA
Clarification questions:
  - Kun tirmaash (quarter) ko installment ho ra estimated annual tax kati ho?
Example user inputs: income tax installment tireko yo tirmaash ko, advance tax jama gareko 15000
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: income_tax_installment, debit: [Income Tax Advance], credit: [Bank Account], amount: 15000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Income Tax Advance (Asset) [amount] / Cr Bank Account [amount]; adjusted against final tax liability at year end
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Confirm installment schedule, due dates, and calculation basis per current Income Tax Act provisions with CA

title: TAX-08 — Excise/Customs Duty
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — TAX-08 Excise/Customs Duty
Category: TAX (Tax/Compliance)
English meaning: Duty paid on imported goods or on domestically produced excisable goods
Nepali meaning: आयातित सामान वा घरेलु उत्पादित उत्पाद कर योग्य सामानमा तिरिने महसुल
Romanized examples: customs duty tireko import ko lagi, excise duty tireko
English examples: Paid customs duty for imported goods, Paid excise duty on manufactured product
Required fields: amount
Optional fields: import_reference, hs_code
Journal entry rule: Dr Inventory/Fixed Asset (capitalized as part of landed cost) [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
Inventory rule: Customs/excise duty is capitalized into the cost of the imported or manufactured goods
Tax rule placeholder: Rates and applicability must be confirmed from the Customs Department and Excise Act, which vary by product category and change periodically
Clarification questions:
  - HS code ra applicable duty rate confirm gareko cha?
Example user inputs: customs duty tireko import ko lagi 45000, excise duty tireko
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: customs_excise_duty, debit: [Inventory/Fixed Asset], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 45000}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Inventory/Fixed Asset (capitalized as part of landed cost) [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
inventory_rule: Customs/excise duty is capitalized into the cost of the imported or manufactured goods
tax_rule_placeholder: Rates and applicability must be confirmed from the Customs Department and Excise Act, which vary by product category and change periodically

title: TAX-09 — Tax Penalty/Fine
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — TAX-09 Tax Penalty/Fine
Category: TAX (Tax/Compliance)
English meaning: A penalty or fine paid to the tax authority for late filing, late payment, or non-compliance
Nepali meaning: ढिलो फाइलिंग, ढिलो भुक्तानी वा नियम पालना नगरेको कारण कर प्राधिकरणलाई तिरिने जरिवाना
Romanized examples: late filing ko lagi penalty tireko, tax office le fine lagayo
English examples: Paid a penalty for late VAT filing, Tax office imposed a fine
Required fields: amount
Optional fields: reason, notice_reference
Journal entry rule: Dr Tax Penalty Expense [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Tax penalties are generally non-deductible for income tax purposes; confirm current treatment with CA
Clarification questions:
  - Penalty ko exact reason ra notice reference ke ho?
Example user inputs: late filing ko lagi penalty tireko 500, tax office le fine lagayo 500
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: tax_penalty, debit: [Tax Penalty Expense], credit: [Cash/Bank], amount: 500, tax_deductibility: generally_non_deductible}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr Tax Penalty Expense [amount] / Cr Cash/Bank [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Tax penalties are generally non-deductible for income tax purposes; confirm current treatment with CA

title: COR-01 — Wrong Entry Reversal
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — COR-01 Wrong Entry Reversal
Category: COR (Correction)
English meaning: An entry previously posted in error needs to be fully reversed and removed from the books
Nepali meaning: पहिले गल्तीले पोस्ट भएको इन्ट्री पूर्ण रूपमा उल्ट्याई खातबाट हटाउनुपर्ने
Romanized examples: galti entry gareko thiye reverse garnu paryo, wrong post bhayo delete garne
English examples: Made a wrong entry, need to reverse it, Posted incorrectly, needs correction
Required fields: original_transaction_reference
Optional fields: reason_for_reversal
Journal entry rule: Post an exact opposite entry to the original (Dr becomes Cr and vice versa) with a clear reference linking to the original transaction, rather than physically deleting historical records
Inventory rule: Reverse whatever inventory effect the original entry had
Tax rule placeholder: If the original entry affected VAT or TDS figures already filed, confirm correction procedure with CA before amending filed returns
Clarification questions:
  - Kun original transaction ho, date/amount/reference confirm garnu hola
Example user inputs: galti entry gareko thiye reverse garnu paryo, wrong post bhayo delete garne
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: entry_reversal, action: post_opposite_entry, reference: original_transaction_id}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Post an exact opposite entry to the original (Dr becomes Cr and vice versa) with a clear reference linking to the original transaction, rather than physically deleting historical records
inventory_rule: Reverse whatever inventory effect the original entry had
tax_rule_placeholder: If the original entry affected VAT or TDS figures already filed, confirm correction procedure with CA before amending filed returns

title: COR-02 — Duplicate Entry Detection
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — COR-02 Duplicate Entry Detection
Category: COR (Correction)
English meaning: A suspicion or confirmation that the same transaction has been recorded more than once
Nepali meaning: एउटै कारोबार एकभन्दा बढी पटक दर्ता भएको शंका वा पुष्टि
Romanized examples: duita entry gareko jasto lagcha aja ko sale ko, same bill number ma pheri entry aayo
English examples: I think I entered today's sale twice, Same bill number appears entered again
Required fields: suspected_duplicate_details
Optional fields: bill_number, date, amount
Journal entry rule: Do not post any reversal until the duplicate is confirmed by comparing entry logs, bill numbers, dates, and amounts; upon confirmation, use COR-01 to reverse the duplicate
Inventory rule: If confirmed duplicate involved inventory movement, reverse the duplicated inventory effect as well
Tax rule placeholder: If duplicate affected already-filed VAT figures, confirm correction procedure with CA
Clarification questions:
  - Kripaya duplicate suspected entries ko date, amount, ra bill number compare gari confirm garnu hola
Example user inputs: duita entry gareko jasto lagcha aja ko sale ko, same bill number ma pheri entry aayo
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: duplicate_check, action: hold_for_verification, status: pending_confirmation}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Do not post any reversal until the duplicate is confirmed by comparing entry logs, bill numbers, dates, and amounts; upon confirmation, use COR-01 to reverse the duplicate
inventory_rule: If confirmed duplicate involved inventory movement, reverse the duplicated inventory effect as well
tax_rule_placeholder: If duplicate affected already-filed VAT figures, confirm correction procedure with CA

title: COR-03 — Edit Transaction
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — COR-03 Edit Transaction
Category: COR (Correction)
English meaning: A request to modify a field (such as amount, party name, or date) on a previously recorded transaction
Nepali meaning: पहिले दर्ता भएको कारोबारको कुनै फिल्ड (जस्तै रकम, पक्षको नाम वा मिति) संशोधन गर्ने अनुरोध
Romanized examples: yo entry ko amount galat cha thik garne, customer ko naam change garnu paryo transaction ma
English examples: The amount on this entry is wrong, please fix it, Need to correct the customer name on this transaction
Required fields: transaction_reference, field_to_edit, new_value
Optional fields: reason
Journal entry rule: If the amount changes, post an adjusting entry for the difference rather than silently altering historical records, to preserve an audit trail
Inventory rule: If the edit affects quantity or value, adjust Inventory correspondingly
Tax rule placeholder: If the edit affects a VAT or TDS figure already filed, confirm amendment procedure with CA
Clarification questions:
  - Kun field change garne ho ra sahi value kati ho?
Example user inputs: yo entry ko amount galat cha thik garne, customer ko naam change garnu paryo transaction ma
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: edit_transaction, action: post_adjusting_entry, reference: transaction_id, field: amount}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: If the amount changes, post an adjusting entry for the difference rather than silently altering historical records, to preserve an audit trail
inventory_rule: If the edit affects quantity or value, adjust Inventory correspondingly
tax_rule_placeholder: If the edit affects a VAT or TDS figure already filed, confirm amendment procedure with CA

title: COR-04 — Delete Transaction Request
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — COR-04 Delete Transaction Request
Category: COR (Correction)
English meaning: A request to remove a previously recorded transaction entirely from the books
Nepali meaning: पहिले दर्ता भएको कारोबार पूर्ण रूपमा खाताबाट हटाउने अनुरोध
Romanized examples: yo entry delete garnu paryo galat thiyo, transaction hataunu paryo system bata
English examples: This entry needs to be deleted, it was wrong, Need to remove this transaction from the system
Required fields: transaction_reference
Optional fields: reason
Journal entry rule: For audit trail integrity, prefer reversing the entry (COR-01) over physically deleting it, especially once a fiscal period has been closed or reported; true deletion should only be allowed for same-day, unreported entries
Inventory rule: Reverse any inventory effect if the entry is removed or reversed
Tax rule placeholder: If the transaction has already been included in a filed VAT/tax return, physical deletion is not appropriate — use a formal reversal/amendment process with CA guidance
Clarification questions:
  - Yo transaction kunai already-filed report/return ma include vaisakeko cha ki chaina?
Example user inputs: yo entry delete garnu paryo galat thiyo, transaction hataunu paryo system bata
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: delete_request, action: reverse_preferred_over_delete, reference: transaction_id}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: For audit trail integrity, prefer reversing the entry (COR-01) over physically deleting it, especially once a fiscal period has been closed or reported; true deletion should only be allowed for same-day, unreported entries
inventory_rule: Reverse any inventory effect if the entry is removed or reversed
tax_rule_placeholder: If the transaction has already been included in a filed VAT/tax return, physical deletion is not appropriate — use a formal reversal/amendment process with CA guidance

title: COR-05 — Reclassify Expense
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — COR-05 Reclassify Expense
Category: COR (Correction)
English meaning: Moving an amount from one expense (or other) account to a more appropriate account without changing the total amount
Nepali meaning: कुल रकम परिवर्तन नगरी एउटा खर्च (वा अन्य) खाताबाट अझ उपयुक्त खातामा रकम सार्ने
Romanized examples: yo kharcha miscellaneous ma galat cha transport ma jane ho, expense category change garnu paryo
English examples: This expense is wrongly in miscellaneous, should be transport, Need to change the expense category
Required fields: transaction_reference, current_account, new_account
Optional fields: reason
Journal entry rule: Dr [New Expense Account] [amount] / Cr [Old Expense Account] [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect typically, unless reclassifying between inventory-related and non-inventory-related accounts
Tax rule placeholder: Reclassification among expense categories generally does not change total taxable profit, but confirm if it affects TDS category treatment
Clarification questions:
  - Kun account bata kun account ma sarne ho?
Example user inputs: yo kharcha miscellaneous ma galat cha transport ma jane ho, expense category change garnu paryo
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: reclassify_expense, debit: [New Account], credit: [Old Account], amount: unchanged}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr [New Expense Account] [amount] / Cr [Old Expense Account] [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect typically, unless reclassifying between inventory-related and non-inventory-related accounts
tax_rule_placeholder: Reclassification among expense categories generally does not change total taxable profit, but confirm if it affects TDS category treatment

title: COR-06 — Change Payment Mode
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — COR-06 Change Payment Mode
Category: COR (Correction)
English meaning: Correcting the recorded payment method for a transaction, such as changing it from cash to bank
Nepali meaning: कारोबारको लागि दर्ता भएको भुक्तानी माध्यम सुधार गर्ने, जस्तै नगदबाट बैंकमा परिवर्तन गर्ने
Romanized examples: yo cash ma post gareko thiye tara bank bata thiyo, payment mode galat cha wallet ma thiyo cash hoina
English examples: This was posted as cash but it was actually bank, Payment mode is wrong, it was wallet not cash
Required fields: transaction_reference, current_payment_mode, correct_payment_mode
Optional fields: reason
Journal entry rule: Dr [Correct Payment Mode Account] [amount] / Cr [Incorrect Payment Mode Account] [amount]
Inventory rule: No inventory effect from this correction alone
Tax rule placeholder: Not applicable — this correction does not change the underlying revenue/expense recognition
Clarification questions:
  - Sahi payment mode ke ho?
Example user inputs: yo cash ma post gareko thiye tara bank bata thiyo, payment mode galat cha wallet ma thiyo cash hoina
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: change_payment_mode, debit: [Correct Account], credit: [Incorrect Account], amount: unchanged}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: Dr [Correct Payment Mode Account] [amount] / Cr [Incorrect Payment Mode Account] [amount]
inventory_rule: No inventory effect from this correction alone
tax_rule_placeholder: Not applicable — this correction does not change the underlying revenue/expense recognition

title: COR-07 — Add Missing Bill Number
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — COR-07 Add Missing Bill Number
Category: COR (Correction)
English meaning: Attaching a bill or invoice reference number to a transaction that was originally recorded without one
Nepali meaning: पहिले बिल नम्बर बिना दर्ता भएको कारोबारमा बिल वा इन्भोइस सन्दर्भ नम्बर थप्ने
Romanized examples: bill number chutey pani sale post gareko thiye pheri add garne, invoice number thapna paryo purano entry ma
English examples: Bill number was missing, need to add it now, Need to attach invoice number to old entry
Required fields: transaction_reference, bill_number
Journal entry rule: No new debit/credit entry required; simply update the reference field on the existing transaction record
Inventory rule: No inventory effect
Tax rule placeholder: Adding a proper bill number retroactively may be important for VAT documentation compliance; confirm any restrictions with CA
Clarification questions:
  - Kun transaction ho ra bill number ke ho?
Example user inputs: bill number chutey pani sale post gareko thiye pheri add garne, invoice number thapna paryo purano entry ma
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: add_bill_reference, action: update_field, field: bill_number}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: No new debit/credit entry required; simply update the reference field on the existing transaction record
inventory_rule: No inventory effect
tax_rule_placeholder: Adding a proper bill number retroactively may be important for VAT documentation compliance; confirm any restrictions with CA

title: COR-08 — Convert Estimate to Invoice
content:
PHASE 4 INTENT TAXONOMY — COR-08 Convert Estimate to Invoice
Category: COR (Correction)
English meaning: A previously issued price quotation or estimate is now being converted into a formal sales invoice because the customer confirmed the order
Nepali meaning: पहिले जारी गरिएको मूल्य अनुमान वा इस्टिमेट अब औपचारिक बिक्री इन्भोइसमा परिवर्तन गरिने, ग्राहकले अर्डर पुष्टि गरेपछि
Romanized examples: estimate lai invoice ma convert garne customer le confirm garya, quotation ko order confirm bhayo bill banaune
English examples: Convert the estimate to an invoice, customer confirmed, Quotation order is confirmed, generate the bill
Required fields: estimate_reference, final_amount
Optional fields: any_changes_from_original_estimate
Journal entry rule: No journal entry exists for the estimate itself (estimates are not transactions); upon conversion, post the actual sale per SAL-01 or SAL-02 rules based on payment terms
Inventory rule: Reduce Inventory only at the point of actual invoice conversion, not at the estimate stage
Tax rule placeholder: VAT/tax treatment applies only from the point of invoice issuance, not from the estimate date
Clarification questions:
  - Final confirmed amount kati ho, estimate bhanda farak cha ki milcha?
Example user inputs: estimate lai invoice ma convert garne customer le confirm garya, quotation ko order confirm bhayo bill banaune
Example ERP output: {transaction_type: estimate_to_invoice_conversion, action: create_new_sale_entry, reference: estimate_id}
source: Nepal SME ERP Phase 4 Intent Taxonomy (AI Training)
journal_entry_rule: No journal entry exists for the estimate itself (estimates are not transactions); upon conversion, post the actual sale per SAL-01 or SAL-02 rules based on payment terms
inventory_rule: Reduce Inventory only at the point of actual invoice conversion, not at the estimate stage
tax_rule_placeholder: VAT/tax treatment applies only from the point of invoice issuance, not from the estimate date
